coveo:metadata.title: Egg Drop Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken stock 4 cups; Salt 3/4 tsp; Green onions 1 bunch; White Pepper 1/8 tsp; Eggs 3 pieces; Turmeric 1/2 tsp; Monosodium Glutamate (MSG) 1/4 tsp; Sugar 1/8 tsp; Cornstarch 3 tbsp; Sesame oil 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Bring the chicken stock to a simmer in a medium pot then stir in the sesame oil, salt, sugar, white pepper, turmeric and MSG (if using). Taste the soup, and adjust the seasoning to your taste. | Next add the cornstarch and water mixture. Stir the soup continuously as you drizzle in the cornstarch slurry to make sure there are no clumps. | Use a spoon to stir the soup and slowly drizzle in all of the beaten eggs until until ribbons form. | Pour the soup into bowls and garnish with green onions, then serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Filipino Arroz Caldo
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Boneless Chicken 1 1/2 lbs; Vegetable oil 2 tbsp; Fish sauce 2 tbsp; White rice 1 1/2 cups; Garlic 1 tsp; Black Pepper 1/4 tsp; Onion 1 cup; Lemon 1 piece; Green onion 1 bunch; Ginger 2 tbsp; Chicken bouillon 1 cube; Safflower or Turmeric 3 tbsp; Water 1 liter; Hard Boiled Egg 4 eggs
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a soup pot, heat the oil then saute the garlic, onion, and ginger until softened. | Next, add in ground black pepper to taste and chicken bouillon cube then cook until the chicken bouillon cube dissolves. | Add the boneless chicken pieces and cook until browned. | Add the fish sauce and uncooked rice then mix well. | Add the water in, making sure everything is covered, and bring to a low boil. Stir occasionally and simmer until the rice is fully cooked. (approximately 20-30 minutes) | Stir in the safflower or turmeric for additional color and flavor. | Garnish with sliced hard boiled eggs, fried garlic, green onions, and lemon, then serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Tom Kha Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Shrimp 10 ounces; Water 3 cups; Chicken Stock 1/2 cup; Lemongrass 2 stalks; Galangal 1.5 cm; Kaffir lime leaves 5 pieces; Red Thai chili 2 pieces; Garlic 3 cloves; Oyster mushrooms 4 ounces; Roma tomato 1 piece; White onion 1/2 piece; Sugar 1 tsp; Fish sauce 3 tbsp; Lime 3 tbsp; Cilantro 1 tsp; Coconut milk 1 can
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Using a mortar and pestle, or similar, bash the garlic, chilli and lemongrass so they burst open to release flavour then add into a soup pot. | Add the kaffir lime leaves, galangal, chicken stock and water to the pot. Simmer on high heat, cover, then reduce to medium heat and simmer for 10 minutes. | Strain the broth, discard the aromatics, then return broth into same pot over low heat. | Add onions, mushrooms, tomatoes, shrimp and simmer 2 minutes or until just cooked. Next stir in sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, and coconut milk. Adjust the seasonings to your liking. | Ladle into bowls and serve with fresh cilantro and more Thai chili!

coveo:metadata.title: Tofu with Century Egg and Pork Floss
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Century eggs 3 pieces; Soft tofu 300 grams; Peanuts 30 grams; Pork floss 10 grams; Cilantro 1 bunch; Garlic 3 cloves; Green onions 1 bunch; Vegetable oil 2 tsp; Preserved vegetables 30 grams; Light soy sauce 2 tsp; Oyster sauce 2 tsp; Sugar 1 tsp; Water 60 ml; Cornstarch 1 1/2 tsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a sauce pan on medium heat, add oil and add the garlic and white parts of green onions. Cook until garlic is golden then remove it from the pan and set aside. | Combine light soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, cornstarch and 60 ml water. | Using the same pan, add the chopped preserved vegetables and stir fry for 1 minute then pour in soy sauce mixture. Cook until sauce has thickened and add 1 tsp sesame oil. | Place the tofu on a serving plate and arrange the century eggs, pork floss, and peanuts on top of the tofu. Drizzle the sauce and garnish with green onions and cilantro.

coveo:metadata.title: Taiwanese Sesame Oil Chicken Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken Legs 3 pounds; Black sesame oil 3 tbsp; Ginger 3 ounces; Michiu (Taiwanese rice wine) 750 ml; Rock sugar 20 grams; Salt 1 tsp; Green onions 2-3 stalks
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat your wok or skillet over medium heat, then add the black sesame oil along with the sliced ginger and cook for 2 minutes. | Turn up the heat to high, and sear the chicken legs (in batches if needed) until browned on all sides. Once the chicken legs are nicely browned, turn the heat back down to medium and add in rice wine, rock sugar, and water. | Cover and simmer over medium heat for 30 minutes. The soup should be bubbling, but not boiling. | The soup is ready once the chicken legs are tender. Season with salt to taste and garnish with scallions when serving.

coveo:metadata.title: Taro Coconut Sago
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Sago Pearls 3/4 cup; Coconut Milk 1 1/2 cup; Sugar 1/3 cup; Whole milk 1 1/2 cup; Taro root 1 1/4 cup; Grass jelly 1/2 can; Yam and taro balls 1 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a pot over medium heat, combine coconut milk, milk, and sugar. | Bring mixture to a boil and remove from heat. Set side. | Peel taro root then cut into slices and using a cookie cutter press out shapes. (Or dice into small 1/2-inch cubes) Steam until tender and a fork or toothpick pricks through, about 10 – 15 minutes and set aside. | In a small pot, cook sago pearls according to instructions on the package then set aside. | Dice grass jelly into small cubes. | Add all the prepared toppings to the coconut milk base. Stir to combine and enjoy! (Can be served hot or cold; chill for 2-4 hours if serving cold)

coveo:metadata.title: Thai Basil Pork Stir Fry (Pad Kra Pao)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Fish sauce 1 tbsp; Light soy sauce 1 tbsp; Kecap manis (Indonesian soy sauce)1 tbsp; Garlic 4 cloves; Oyster sauce 1 tbsp; Water 4 tbsp; Green beans 5.5 ounces; Minced pork 7 ounces; Basil 1/2 bunch; Red Thai Chili 3 pieces; Eggs 1-2 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Mix together the fish sauce, soy sauce, kecap manis, oyster sauce and water in a bowl or jug and set aside. | Heat up a large drizzle of oil in a wok or stir-fry pan. Then stir-fry the green beans for a few minutes on a medium high heat. Add the chopped chilis and garlic and stir-fry for a couple more minutes. | Add the pork and break up with a wooden spoon. Stir-fry until cooked (an additional few minutes). | Pour in the sauce, stir well, then let bubble for a minute or two more. | Stir in the basil until wilted, then serve with the rice and a fried egg.

coveo:metadata.title: Beef Chow Fun
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Flank Steak 8 ounces; Baking Soda 1/4 tsp; Ginger 1 piece; Cornstarch 1 tsp; Shaoxing wine 2 tbsp; Green Onions 4 stalks; Wide Rice Noodles 12 ounces; Dark Soy Sauce 2 tsp; Vegetable Oil 1 tsp + 3 tsp; Bean Sprouts 5 ounces; Light Soy Sauce 1 tsp + 2 tbsp; Sugar 1/8 tsp; Sesame Oil 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a medium bowl mix together the flank steak, baking soda, cornstarch, vegetable oil, and soy sauce then marinate for 1 hour. | Heat up vegetable oil in a large skillet then cook the marinated beef until it has browned and remove from the pan. | Using the same pan add in ginger, green onions, wide rice noodles, Shaoxing wine, sesame oil, dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, cooked beef, sugar, and bean sprouts. Mix well until everything is incorporated. | Add salt and white pepper to taste. | Serve and garnish with more green onions.

coveo:metadata.title: Sliced Pork with Garlic Sauce
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork Belly 1 pound; Water 6 cups; Ginger 5 slices; Star anise 1 piece; Shaoxing wine 2 tbsp; Green Onions 3 stalks; Sichuan peppercorns 2 tsp; Red Chili Flakes 2 tbsp; Vegetable Oil ⅓ cup; Garlic 10 cloves; Light Soy Sauce 3½ tbsp; Black Vinegar 1 tbsp; Sugar 1 tbsp; Sesame Oil 1 tsp; Sesame Paste 1 tbsp; Cucumber 1 piece; Toasted Sesame Seeds 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large pot add the water, pork belly, ginger, star anise, Shaoxing wine, Sichuan peppercorns, and green onions then boil. | When the pork belly has cooked remove it from the pot and reserve 2 cups of the cooking liquid. | Slice the cooked pork belly. | Using a small bowl add in the minced garlic, ginger, green onions, chili flakes, sichuan peppercorns, boiling oil, soy sauce, black vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, sesame paste, reserved cooking liquid, and toasted sesame seeds then mix. | Place the cucumber slices in a large bowl then layer the slices of pork on top. | Pour the sauce over the pork and cucumbers, then garnish with green onions.

coveo:metadata.title: Braised Pork Belly
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork Belly 2 lbs; Green Onion 1 bunch; Ginger Few Slices; Mogami Canola Olive Oil 2 Tbsp; Simbo Yellow Rock Sugar 1 Tbsp; Asian Taste Shaoxing Cooking Wine 3 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 1 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce 1/2 Tbsp; Water 2 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut pork belly into desired size. Bring water to boil and add in few slices of ginger, few stems of green onion, and pork belly. Cook the pork belly until it turn white on the surface and take it out of the pot. | In a wok, with medium high heat, add in Vegeable Oil, Rock Sugar, and Pork Belly. Cook Until the pork belly turn brown. | Add in Shaoxing Wine, Soy Sauce and Dark Soy Sauce and cook for a few minutes | Add in Water, with medium low heat, cover with lid and simmer for 1 hour. Until most liquids are evaporated. | Ready to serve in your favorite dinnerware, and sprinkle some chopped Green Onion as garnish.

coveo:metadata.title: Hot & Sour Napa Cabbage
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Napa Cabbage 1 piece; Salt 1 tsp; Red Chili Flakes 1 tsp; Soy Sauce 2 tsp; Oyster Sauce 1 tsp; Red Thai Chili 4-6 pieces; Garlic 3 cloves; Green Onions 2 stalks; Vegetable Oil 3 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Remove napa cabbage root, split in half & cut into equal parts. | Rinse with water, add salt, and mix. | Let it sit for 20 minutes & drain water out. | Add in the red chili flakes, soy sauce, oyster sauce, Thai chilis, garlic, green onions, and boiling oil then mix to combine.

coveo:metadata.title: Chicken Teriyaki
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Boneless Chicken Thigh 4 pcs; Onion 1/4 pcs; Ginger Desire Amount; Honbiou Cooking Wine 1 Tbsp; Wan Ja Shan Mirin 1 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 2 Tbsp; C&H Sugar 1 Tbsp; Water 2 Tbsp; Salt Desire Amount; Pepper Desire Amount
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl, mix in ginger, onion, cooking wine, mirin, soy sauce, sugar, and water. | Use a fork to poke through the chicken thign. Season with salt & pepper on both sides and place inside the bowl of marinate. | In a pan, add oil with medium high heat, please the chicken thigh with the skin side down first. Flip to the otherside and repeat until the chicken is cooked. | Remove the chicken to the side. Clean out the pan residue. And place the chicke thigh back in the pan, pour in the remaining marinate, cook with medium heat until all the marinate evaoprates | Ready to serve. Eat alone or serve over rice and vegeatble as a complete meal

coveo:metadata.title: Ginger Soy Fish
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: White fish 12 ounces; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Green Onions 1 piece; Sugar 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Cornstarch 1 tbsp; Vegetable Oil 2 tbsp; White Pepper 1 tsp; Water 2 tbsp; Ginger 1 piece
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut the white fish into cubes and coat evenly in cornstarch, then set aside. | Using a different bowl add in soy sauce, sesame oil, water, sugar, and white pepper then mix well with a whisk. | In a medium pan heat up the vegestable oil with the ginger slices until it has browned and set aside. | Fry the fish in the oil that was used to brown the ginger until each piece is crispy. | When the fish has browned add in the soy sauce mixture and stir to coat. | Remove the fish from the heat then serve with green onions and the crispy ginger.

coveo:metadata.title: Beijing Beef
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Beef Flank Steak 1 lbs; Red Bell Pepper 1 pcs; Green Bell Pepper 1 pcs; Onion 1 pcs; Garlic To desire amount; Egg Whites 3 pcs; Salt 1/4 tsp; Flying Horse Tapioca Starch 3 Tbsp; Water 1/2 cup; C&H Sugar 1/4 cup; Heinz Ketchup 3 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce 6 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Oyster Sauce 2 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Thai Chili Sauce 4 tsp; Simbo Crushed Red Pepper 1 tsp; Kong Yen Rice Vinegar 2 Tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Slice beef to desired thickness. Mix with egg whites and tapioca powder, set it aside for 30 minutes for beef to tenderize | In another bowl, mix in water, sugar, ketchup, hoisin sauce, oyster sauce, thai chili sauce, crushed red pepper and rice vinegar | Coat the beef slices with taipioca powder, and deep fry it with medium high heet until golden brown and set it aside. | Discard the extra oil and only leave proper amount of oil in the pan, add in green bell pepper, red bell pepper, onion and garlic. Stir fy it until onion becomes translucent | In the same pan, add in the condiment mixture and bring to boil, add in the vegetable mix and beef, mix property and ready to serve.

coveo:metadata.title: Vegetarian Egg Roll
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Wei - Chuan Egg Roll Shells 1 pack; Garlic 3 Tbsp; Asian Taste Dry Mushroom 3 Tbsp; Celery 4 oz; Cabbage 8 oz; Carrot 3 oz; Green Onion 3 oz; Cloud Ear Fungus Mushroom 3 oz; Salt 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee White Pepper Powder 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Vegetarian Oyster Sauce 1 tsp; Sinbo Fine Rice Vermicelli 2 oz; Mizuho 100% Pure Sesame Oil 1 Tbsp; Gold Medal Flour 2 Tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a pan with medium high heat, add in oil, garlic, shitake mushroom, celebry, cabbage, carrot, green onion, cloud ear fungus mushroom, salt, sugar, vegetarian oyster sauce, vermicelli noodles, and sesame oil. Stir fry untl the ingredients become soft and set it aside | Take a egg roll wrapper, place proper amount of the vegetable mixture as the filling, roll upwards, fold the two sides and seal the wrapper with 1:1 water/ flour mixture | In a pan with medium high heat and oil, deep fry 2-3 minutes for each side | Egg rolls are now ready to serve. Be carefull of the hot filling!

coveo:metadata.title: Bo Kho
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Three Crabs Fish Sauce 2 Tbsp; Kimbo Brown Sugar 1 Tbsp; Ginger 4 Tbsp; Wu Hsing Five Spice Powder 2 Tbsp; Pepper 1/2 tsp; Beef Chuck 3 lbs; Red Onion 1; Garlic 2 Cloves; Lemongrass 3 Tbsp; Tomato 1; Salt 1 tsp; Tumeric Powder 1/2 Tsp; Water 4 cups; Cinnamon Stick 1; Red Thai Chilies Few; Carrots 1.5 lbs
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl, mix fish sauce, brown sugar, ginger, Chinese 5 spice, and black pepper | In a large bowl, place beef chuck in desire size and mix in the seasoning mix from step 1. Cover the bowl and let it marinate for minimum 30 mins | In a pan, add some vegetable oil, and cookl the beef chuck in batches until all sides are seared, and set the beef aside | In the same pan, add in garlic, lemon grass, tomatic, salt, tumeric powderr, water, cinnamon stick, red Thai chilies. Place the beef back back in the pot. Bring to boil, turn down to medium low heat, cover the pot and simmer for 1 hour | Add in carrots and simmer for 15 minutes | Before serving, remove the cinnamon stick fromt the pot. Serve alone or with baguette/ rice

coveo:metadata.title: Braised Eggplant with Garlic Sauce
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chinese Eggplant 2; Flying Horse Tapioca Starch 3 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 2 tsp; Asian Taste Chinkiang Vinegar 1 Tbsp; Water 1/2 Cup; C&H Sugar 2 Tbsp; Salt 1/4 Tsp; Ground Pork 1/4 lb; Lee Kum Kee Chili Bean Sauce 1 Tbsp; Garlic 3 Cloves; Ginger 2 Tbsp; LGM Pickled Chili 1 Tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Chop the eggplant to desired size. Coated with corn starch and set aside | Mix soy sauce, black vinegar, water, sugar, salt and corn starch together to make the sauce mixture | In a skillet, with medium high heat and vegetable oil, pan fry the eggplant until it's golden brown on both sides. Take the eggplant out and set it aside | In the same skillet, add in ground pork, chili bean paste, garlic, ginger and pickled pepper, stir fry until the pork is all the way cooked | Pour in the sauce mixture, add in the eggplant and mix until the sauce is evenly coated on the eggplant | Ready to serve. Optional, sprinkle some chopped green onion as garnish

coveo:metadata.title: Honey Walnut Shrimp
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Blue Diamond Shrimp 1 lb; Water 1/2 Cup; C&H Sugar 1/2 Cup; Walnut Halves 1/2 Cup; Honey 1 1/2 tsp; Kewpie Mayo 3 Tbsp; Carnation Condensed Milk 1 1/2 tsp; Salt Proper Amount; Pepper Proper Amount; Egg 1; Flying Horse Tapioca Starch 1/2 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a skillet, add in equal parts of water and sugar with medium low heat. Stir until the sugar is fully dissolved and add in the walnut halves. Evenly coat the syrup mix around the walnut halves, and set it aside | In a small bowl, mix honey, mayoonaise, condense milk together to make the glaze mixture | In a bowl, mix peeled and deveined shrimp, mix with salt, pepper and egg. Coat the each shrimp evenly with corn starch | In a skillet with medium high heat, fry the shrimp until it's golden brown and set it aside | In a bowl, mix the shrimp, glaze mixture, and evently coat the shrimp with the glaze mixture. Add in candied walnut, and it's ready to eat!

coveo:metadata.title: Kung Pao Chicken
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken 2 lbs; Asian Taste Shaoxing Cooking Wine 3 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 6 Tbsp; Baking Soda 1 1/2 tsp; Kimbow Chicken Broth 1/2 Cup; Asian Taste Chinkiang Vinegar 2 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce 2 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Hoisin Sauce 2 tsp; C&H Sugar 3 Tbsp; Flying Horse Tapioca Starch 1 1/2 tsp; Garlic 3 Cloves; Ginger 1 1/2Tbsp; Red Bell Pepper 1 pcs; Green Bell Pepper 1 pcs; Sichuan Peppercorns 1/2Tbsp; Peanuts 1/2 Cup; Green Onions 1 bunch; Mizuho 100% Pure Sesame Oil 2 Tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl, mix chicken, soy sauce, shaoxing wine, baking soda and corstarch togther. Marinate for 10 minutes | In a seperate bowl, mix chicken stock, soy sauce, vinegar, shaoxing wine, dark soy sauce, hoisin sauce, sugar and cornstarch together as the sauce mix | In a skillet with medium high heat, add the chicken and stir fry until it's fully cooked and set it aside | In the pan with medium high heat, add in oil, garlic, ginger, red bell pepper, green bell pepper, sichuan pepperforns, and the sauce base, and bring it to a boil | Add in chicken, peanuts, and green onion to the pan. Mix well and ready to serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Spicy & Sour Shredded Potato
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Potatoes 2 pcs; Sichuan Peppercorns 2 tsp; Dry Chili Peppers 8 pcs; Green Onions 1 bunch; Garlic 4 Cloves; Red Bell Pepper 1 pcs; C&H Sugar 1 tsp; Salt 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 1 Tbsp; Asian Taste Chinkiang Vinegar 1 Tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Slice potatoes into thin slices. Soak in cold water. Drain and set aside | In a pan with medium high heat, add in oil, sichuan pepperforns, dry chili peppers, green onions (white part only), bellpepper, and shredded potato. Stir fry it | Add in sugar, salt, soy sauce, vinegar and scallion. Cook until it's tender and it's ready to serve

coveo:metadata.title: Braised Chinese Mushroom with baby Bok Choy
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Asian Taste Dry Mushroom 2 1/2 oz; Baby Bok Choy 10 oz; Garlic 4 Cloves; Asian Taste Shaoxing Cooking Wine 1 Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Premium Dark Soy Sauce 1Tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Soy Sauce 1 1/2 tsp; Mizuho 100% Pure Sesame Oil 1/8 tsp; C&H Sugar 1 1/2 tsp; Lee Kum Kee White Pepper Powder 1/8 tsp; Flying Horse Tapioca Starch 1 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Place the dry mushroom in a bowl, our in boiling hot water, soak for 3 minutes | Drain the Mushroom water and save it | In another pot, bring water to boil, add in vegetable oil, and salt, quickly blanch the bak choy and move the bak choy to cold water | In a skillet, add in vegetable oil, garlic, and mushrooms. And add in shaoxing wine, mushroom water, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, sugar, white pepper and the cornstarch slurry | Bring to boil and ready to eat! Pro tip: follow the video to see the plating instrustion

coveo:metadata.title: Mapo Tofu
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Vegetable Oil 1/4 cup & 1/4 cup; Dried Red Chili 10 pieces; Thai Chili 2 pieces; Ginger 3 tbsp; Garlic 4 tbsp; Ground Pork 1 1/2 pounds; Sichuan Peppercorn 1/2 tsp; Spicy Bean Sauce 2 tbsp; Chicken Stock 1 cup; Cornstarch 1 1/2 tsp; Water 1 1/2 tsp; Silken Tofu 1 pound; Sesame Oil 1/4 tsp; Sugar 1/4 tsp; Green Onion 1 piece
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Begin by heating the vegetable oil in a large skillet, then add in the dried red chilis and the Thai chilis. Toast the chilis then set aside. | Next add more oil to the pan along with ginger, garlic, and ground pork. Make sure to break up the ground pork as it cooks. | When the pork has browned add in Sichuan peppercorns, spicy bean sauce, and chicken stock then bring to a simmer. | Add the cornstarch slurry and the toasted chilis to the mixture and stir until thickened. | Add in the silken tofu, sesame oil, sugar, and green onions then stir to coat everything in the sauce. | Serve over rice with extra green onions for garnish.

coveo:metadata.title: Stir Fry Clams in Black Bean Sauce
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Fresh Clams 1/2 lbs.; Vegetable Oil 1 tsp; Green Bell Pepper 1 piece; Red Bell Pepper 1 piece; Ginger 2 tbsp; Green Onion 2 stalks; Garlic 2 cloves; Fermented Black Beans 1 tbsp; Shaoxing Wine 1 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp; White Pepper 1/8 tsp; Sesame Oil 1 tsp; Cornstarch 1 tbsp; Water 1 tbsp; Cilantro 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a large pan with ginger, garlic, green onion, bell peppers, and fermented black beans. | Saute over medium heat and stir. | Add in uncooked fresh clams and shaoxing wine to the pan. | Cook the clams until they open to remove any fishy odor. | Add in sugar, white pepper, sesame oil, and cornstarch slurry. | Stir to combine and top with chopped cilantro.

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Lion's Head Meatball
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Green Onions 2 stalks; Minced Pork 400 grams; Minced Chicken 100 grams; Shaoxing Wine 2 tbsp; Light soy sauce 2 tbsp; Salt ½ tsp; Sugar ½ tsp; Sesame oil ½ tsp; Eggs 1 piece; Cornstarch 1 tbsp; Water Chestnut 60 grams; Vegetable Oil (for frying)1-2 cups; Napa Cabbage 7 pieces; Dried Shitake Mushroom 6 pieces; Dried Shrimp ½ tbsp; White Pepper ¼ tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Prepare a tasty meatball mixture by combining minced pork, minced chicken, scallions, Shaoxing rice wine, light soy sauce, salt, sugar, sesame oil, egg, cornstarch, and minced water chestnuts. Mix well until sticky. | Divide the mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into a ball. | Deep fry the meatballs until golden brown and cooked through. Set aside. | In a separate pot, use the water in which dried shiitake mushrooms were soaked to simmer the fried meatballs, Napa cabbage, soaked mushrooms, dried shrimp (if using), salt, and ground white pepper. Simmer until cabbage is tender. | Serve the Lion’s Head Meatballs hot with the aromatic broth and enjoy the flavors of prosperity and good fortune!

coveo:metadata.title: Pork Char Siu
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork Shoulder 12 ounces; Char Siu Sauce 4 tbsp; Oyster Sauce 1 tbsp; Light Soy Sauce 1/2 tbsp; Chinese 5 Spice Powder 1/4 tsp; Chili Powder 1/2 tsp; Garlic 6 cloves; Ginger 8 pieces; Honey 2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large bowl whisk together Char Siu sauce, oyster sauce, light soy sauce, Chinese 5 Spice powder, chili powder, garlic, ginger, and honey until well combined. | Place the pork shoulder in a gallon size freezer bag. | Pour the Char Siu sauce mixture over the pork shoulder and close the bag. (Make sure to save some for basting) | Massage the mixture into the pork shoulder and refridgerate for 6 hours. | Place the pork on a baking rack over a cooking tray and fill the bottom of the tray with water. | Roast in the oven for 450F for 30-45 minutes. | Baste every 10-15 minutes with the remaining sauce. | Let the pork cool, then slice it and serve with rice.

coveo:metadata.title: Oyako Don
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken Thighs 12 ounces; Sake 1 tbsp; Eggs 4; Dashi Stock 1/2 cup; Soy Sauce 2 tbsp; Mirin 2 tbsp; Sugar 2 tsp; Onion 1/2; Green Onion 2 stalks; Parsley 1 tsp; Shichimi Togarashi 1/2 tsp; Sansho Pepper Powder 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Marinate chicken thigh pieces in sake for 5 minutes. | In a seperate bowl crack 4 eggs and mix, then set aside. | Mix dashi stock, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar in a small bowl. | Add sliced onions and dashi mixture to a large skillet, then bring it to a boil. | When the mixture has begun to boil add in the sake marinated chicken thighs. | Pour over the beaten eggs and cover with a lid until the eggs have set. | Top with chopped green onions and plate over rice. | Garnish with chopped parsley, shichimi togarashi, and sansho pepper powder.

coveo:metadata.title: Lu Rou Fan
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork Belly 1 pound; Vegetable Oil 1 tbsp; Rock Sugar 1 ounce; Garlic 4 cloves; Shallots 2 pieces; Shitake Mushrooms 8 pieces; Chinese 5 Spice Powder 1 tsp; Shaoxong Wine 1/4 cup; Light Soy Sauce 3 tbsp; Dark Soy Sauce 2 tbsp; Water 2 cups; Star Anise 4 pieces; Szechuan Peppercorn 2 tsp; Bay Leaves 2 pieces; Cloves 6-8 pieces; Cinnamon Stick 1 piece; Ginger 6 slices; Orange Peel 1 piece; Hard Boiled Egg 4 eggs
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a wok heat up oil and rock sugar until the sugar begins to disolve, then add garlic and shallots. | When the garlic and shallots begin to soften add in shitake mushrooms, Chinese 5 spice, pork belly, Shaoxing wine, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and water. | Stir and bring to a boil. | Using a cheese cloth, add star anise, Szechuan peppercorns, bay leaves, cloves, cinnamon stick, ginger, and orange then tie off the spice bag. | Add the spice bag and hard boiled eggs to the wok, then cover with a lid letting the mixture simmer for 1-2 hours. Make sure to skim off the excess fat. | Remove the spice bag and serve over rice.

coveo:metadata.title: Hot & Sour Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork Shoulder 4 ounces; Cornstarch 1 tbsp & 1/3 cup; Vegetable Oil 1 tbsp; Water 1 tbsp & 1/2 cup; Chicken Stock 2 quarts; Salt 1/4 tsp; Sugar 1/4 tsp; Dried Chili 3-4 pieces; Dried Shitake Mushroom 1/2 cup; Wood Ear Mushroom 1/2 cup; White Pepper 2 tsp; Light Soy Sauce 1 tbsp; Dark Soy Sauce 2 tsp; Sesame Oil 1 tsp; Bamboo Shoot 4 ounces; Dried Lily Flower 1/2 cup; Firm Tofu 4 ounces; Spiced Dry Tofu 4 ounces; White Vinegar 1/3 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a small bowl mix together pork shoulder pieces, cornstarch, vegetable oil, and water then set aside. | Heat up chicken stock in a large pot and bring it to a boil. | Once the chicken stock is boiling add in the marinated pork shoulder pieces, salt, sugar, dried chilis, white pepper, light soy sauce, sesame oil, dark soy sauce, dried lily flower, dried shitake mushrooms, dried wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, firm tofu, dried spiced tofu, and white vinegar. | Mix all the ingredients well, then slowly add in the cornstarch slurry while continuing to mix. | When the soup has thickened stir in one beaten egg until ribbons form. | Serve in a bowl and garnish with chopped green onions.

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Steamed Fish
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Sea Bass (or fish of your choice) 1 pound; Fermented Black Beans 1 tbsp; Garlic 3 cloves; Ginger 1 piece; Green Onions 2-3 stalks; Vegetable Oil 1 tbsp; Sesame Oil 1/2 tsp; Lemon 1 piece; Cilantro 1 tbsp; Red Thai Chili 2-3 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a small bowl add in the fermented black beans, minced garlic, ginger slices, and green onions then mix together. | Pat dry your cleaned fish with a paper towel to remove the excess moisture. | Stuff the fermented black bean mixture inside the cavity of the fish. | Drizzle the fish with vegetable oil, sesame oil, and the juice of half a lemon. | Steam the fish for 10-15 minutes using a steamer or by creating a steaming rack using foil. | When the fish has finished cooking serve with more green onions, sliced red Thai chilis, and cilantro.

coveo:metadata.title: Beef Pad See Ew
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Flank Steak 8 ounces; Vegetable oil 1 tsp + 4 tbsp; Fish sauce 1 tsp; Garlic 3 cloves; White pepper 1/2 tsp; Chinese broccoli 1 bunch; Cornstarch 1 tsp; Soy sauce 1 tsp + 2 tsp; Dark soy sauce 1 tbsp; Wide rice noodles 1 pound; Sugar 1/2 tsp; Oyster sauce 1 tbsp; Eggs 2 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl add the dark soy sauce, vegetable oil, and cornstarch, to the flank steak and mix until the beef is completely coated. Set aside. | Heat a wok on high and oil evenly to coat the pan. Then sear the steak until it is 80% cooked through, and transfer it back to the marinade bowl. | Add more oil to the wok and stir in the garlic and Chinese broccoli. Make sure to stir constantly to prevent the garlic from burning. | Cook the wide rice noodles according to the package and set aside. (Remember, 8 ounces of dried noodles will rehydrate to 16 ounces.) | Add the cooked noodles and the cooked steak, then stir again. | Push the mixture to one side to let the empty side of the wok heat for 10 seconds. Then add two beaten eggs and scramble. | Next, combine oyster sauce, sugar, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, fish sauce, and white pepper in a small bowl. Pour the sauce into the wok and make sure everything is evenly coated. | Stir-fry the mixture for another 1-2 minutes until everything is mixed well and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Kung Pao Shrimp
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: P&D Shrimp 1 pound; Canola Oil 1 tsp + 1 tsp + 3 tbsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tsp; Salt 1/4 tsp; White pepper 1/8 tsp; Cornstarch 1/2 tsp + 1 tsp; Rice wine vinegar 1 tbsp; Light soy sauce 1 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp; Sichuan peppercorn powder 1/2 tsp; Dark soy sauce 1/2 tsp; Garlic 3 cloves; Ginger 1 tbsp; Dried red chilies 2-4 pieces; Green onions 6 pieces; Raw Peanuts 1 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat 1 teaspoon of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the raw peanuts and toast until golden brown, then set aside. | In a large bowl add the shrimp, oil, Shaoxing wine, salt, and white pepper powder. Then set aside for 15 minutes and mix in the cornstarch right before cooking. | Heat the pan over high heat then add 2 tbsp of oil, followed by the shrimp and quickly sear the shrimp on both sides. Once they turn pink remove from the pan and set aside. | Lower the heat to and add the remaining tablespoon of oil, garlic, ginger, chilies, and scallions. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant. | Increase the heat to high, and add the shrimp back to the wok. Stir-fry for 30 seconds. | In a bowl, combine water, rice wine vinegar, light soy sauce, sugar, cornstarch, Sichuan peppercorn powder, and dark soy sauce. | Add the sauce to the pan, and stir-fry for another minute until it thickens. Next add the peanuts, then mix until everything is coated, and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Hong Kong Style Curry Fish Ball
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Fish Ball 2-3 lbs; Lee Kum Kee Sauce for Hong Kong Style Fish Balls 1 jar; Lee Kum Kee’s Premium Oyster Sauce 1 tbsp; Water 4 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Begin by boiling the frozen fish balls in a wok. | Heat up the wok, add Lee Kum Kee Sauce for Hong Kong Style Fish Balls and stir-fry over medium heat for 2 minutes. | Add Lee Kum Kee’s Premium Oyster Sauce, mix well and then add water. Turn up the heat and bring it to a boil, then add the fish balls. | Turn down the heat and simmer for about 20 minutes (stirring constantly midway to prevent burning). | For an extra kick, add Lee Kum Kee Fiery Hot Dried Shrimp Chili Oil as an additional seasoning.

coveo:metadata.title: Easy Rice Cooker Pork Fried Rice
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: White Rice 3 cups; Tomato (diced) 1 piece; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Soy sauce 3 tbsp; Edamame 1/4 cup; Carrots (diced) 1/4 cup; Mushrooms (diced) 1/4 cup; Corn 1/4 cup; Chinese sausage (diced) 1/4 cup; Salt to taste
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a rice cooker add 3 cups of washed white rice, then cover the rice with 4 1/2 cups of water or until it reaches the number 3 on the water line. | Add in 1 tsp sesame oil, 3 tbsp soy sauce, and salt to the rice then stir. | Now add in the diced tomato, diced Chinese sausage, diced mushroom, diced carrots, corn, and edamame to the rice then cook. | Once the rice cooker finishes it's cooking cycle stir all the ingredients together until everything is mixed well. Serve in a bowl and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Vietnamese Shaking Beef (BÒ LÚC LẮC)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Beef steak 1 lbs; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Oyster sauce 2 tbsp; Minced garlic 4 cloves; Sugar 2 tsp; Yellow onion 1 piece; Green onion 3 stalks; Salt 1/8 tsp; Pepper 1/8 tsp; Neutral cooking oil 2 tbsp; MSG (optional) to taste
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut the beef into 1 inch cubes and add soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, minced garlic, salt, pepper and msg. Mix well and set aside. | In a hot pan add neutral oil and sear the cubes of beef. Make sure the pan is not overcrowded. (Cook the beef in small batches at a time if needed.) | Make sure the beef is seared on all sides by shaking the pan to flip the cubes, or using a spatula or tongs. | Next, add the onions to the beef and continue shaking and stirring until the onions are just tender, about 1-2 minutes. | Serve with rice or over a bed of greens with sliced tomatoes and cucumbers!

coveo:metadata.title: Sriracha Mayo Poke Bruschetta
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: French baguette 1 loaf; Raw Salmon 4 oz; Raw Tuna 4 oz; Imitation crab with mayo (pre-mixed) 3 oz; Avocado (sliced) 1/2 piece; Masago 2 tbsp; Cucumber (sliced) 2 oz; Tomato (sliced) 2 oz; Green onion 1 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee's Premium Oyster Sauce 2 tbsp; Mirin 4 tsp; Rice vinegar 1/2 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Mayo 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut baguette into slices and toast lightly. Add the oyster sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, and sesame oil in a small bowl and mix sauce well, then set aside. | Dice salmon and tuna. | Mix the diced salmon and tuna with green onion and sesame, then stir in the sauce. | Place tomato, avocado, and cucumber slices on toasted baguette. Add fish mixture and imitation crab on top. | Drizzle each with the sauce and Sriracha mayo. Finally, top with masago and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Lobster Noodles (Lobster Yee Mein)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Lobster tail 12 oz; Cornstarch 1 cup + 1 tbsp; Salt 1 tsp; White pepper 1 tsp; Canola oil 1 cup; Oyster sauce 1 1/2 tbsp; Soy sauce 1 1/2 tbsp; Sugar 1/2 tbsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Chicken broth 1/4 cup + 1 cup; Green onion 1 bunch; Garlic 4-5 cloves; Ginger 1 tbsp; Noodles (E-Yu, Yi Mein, or Shanghai noodles) 16 oz; Water 3 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Begin by cutting the lobster tails into 2 inch pieces making sure to leave the shell on. Now mix the sauce. Start by combining oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, white pepper, shaoxing wine, sesame oil, and chicken broth then set aside. | On a large plate mix cornstarch, salt, and white pepper. Then coat the lobster pieces in the seasoned cornstarch, and shake off any excess. | Next, cook the noodles in boiling water. Then after draining, immediately rinse the noodles with cold water to remove excess starch and prevent over cooking. | In a wok or large pan heat 1 cup of canola oil to about 350F then fry the lobster pieces until the lobster is bright red and cooked through. (about 1 minute) | Now in a large pan or wok, add 1 tbsp of oil and stir fry garlic, ginger, and scallion whites until fragrant. Then add in the fried lobster pieces and stir fry. | Add sauce and stir over high heat, then combine chicken stock and heat until boiling, then simmer for 4-5 minutes to reduce. | Add scallion greens then add cornstarch slurry and stir until the sauce has thickened. Mix in noodles in and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Char Siu Pork Fried Rice
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Canola oil 2 tbsp; Shallots (diced) 1 oz; Eggs (beaten) 2 pieces; White rice 1 lbs; Pork Char Siu (diced) 4 oz; Green onion 1 oz; Oyster sauce 2 tbsp; Soy sauce 1 1/2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Using a hot wok, stir-fry diced shallots in 2 tbsp oil until fragrant. | Next, add in beaten eggs to the wok and scramble. | Mix in cooked rice, diced char siu, green onions, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. | Cook until everything has heated through and serve! | Optional Seasoning: Add Lee Kum Kee Fiery Hot Dried Shrimp Chili Oil or Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil as an to enhance the flavor.

coveo:metadata.title: Boiled Seafood Platter
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Shell-On Shrimp 10 oz; Mussels (on half shell) 8 pieces; Squid rings 6 oz; Crab sticks 4 pieces; Fresh scallops 6 oz; Vegetable oil 4 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic 3 tbsp; Minced millet pepper 2 tbsp; Green onion (for garnish) 2 tbsp; Lemon (for garnish) 2 slices; Cilantro (for garnish) 2 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Soy Sauce 6 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cook the seafood in separate batches in boiling water, draining each well and placing onto a plate. | Heat the oil in a pot and quickly stir-fry the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and millet pepper until fragrant. Drizzle the mixture over the cooked seafood. | Scatter chopped scallion, lemon and cilantro over the dish, and drizzle the seafood with Lee Kum Kee Seasoned Soy Sauce for Seafood and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil then serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Spicy Korean Pork Bulgogi
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork belly (sliced) 2 lbs; Gochujang 1/4 cup; Light soy sauce 2 tbsp; Sugar 1 tbsp; Gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) 2 tbsp; Black pepper 1 tsp; Garlic (minced) 4-6 cloves; Ginger (minced) 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tbsp; Yellow onion 1 piece; Green onions 4 stalks; Canola oil 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl mix together the gochujang, light soy sauce, sugar, gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), black pepper, salt, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil. Then set aside. | Next, add in the sliced pork belly, sliced yellow onion, and green onions to the mixture. Stir everything until the pork is covered in the sauce, then marinate it for 15 minutes. | In a large pan heat 1 tbsp of cooking oil over medium heat then add in the pork. Cook the pork for about 3-5 minutes until the pork has cooked through. | Serve over white rice and garnish with extra green onions, then enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Beef Pad Thai
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Kikkoman Noodle Kit with Sauce Pad Thai 1 pack; Canola Oil 2 tsp; Ground Beef ¾ pound; Snap Peas 2 cups; Shredded Carrots 2 cups; Green Onions ½ cup; Peanuts 1 tsp; Limes 1-2 wedges
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Boil noodles until they have soften and then drain. | Heat oil in a large skillet, then cook and crumble beef until browned. | Add snap peas, carrots, and scallions; stir until peas are vibrant. | Mix in sauce, noodles, and half of the reserved cooking liquid then simmer until veggies are tender. | Serve hot with peanuts and lime wedges.

coveo:metadata.title: Thai Duck Salad (Larb Ped)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Duck 12 oz; Lime juice 1 1/2 tbsp; White sugar 1/2 tsp; Fish sauce 1/2 tsp; Red chili flakes 1/2 tsp; Toasted rice powder 1/2 tsp; Green onions 1 bunch; Cilantro 1 bunch; Shallots 2 pieces; Kaffir lime leaves 4-6 pieces; Dried chili 4-6 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Separate the duck meat from the skin, then mince the meat and cut the skin into small pieces. Fry the duck skin until crispy and transfer on to a paper towl to absorb excess grease. | In the duck fat, fry the dried chilies, kaffir lime leaves, and shallots until aromatic. Place the aromatics on a paper towl to drain excess oil and set aside. | Using the same pan add in the minced duck meat and fry until cooked through, then set aside. | In a large bowl mix together lime juice, sugar, fish sauce, red chili flakes, and toasted rice powder. | Add the cooked minced duck meat, green onions, cilantro, and shallots to the dressing. Mix well. | Garnish with the fried dried chilies, fried kaffir lime leaves, fried shallots, crispy duck skin, green onions, and cilantro. Serve jasmine rice, sticky rice, or in lettuce wraps!

coveo:metadata.title: Filipino Salmon in Coconut Milk (Ginataang Salmon)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Salmon steak 1 1/2 lbs; Coconut cream 2 cups; Garlic (minced) 3 cloves; Ginger (minced) 1 tbsp; Long green chili 3 pieces; Red chili 3 pieces; Yellow onion (sliced) 1 piece; Cane vinegar 1/4 cup; Fish sauce 1 tbsp; Ground black pepper 1/4 tsp; Water 1/2 cup; Canola oil 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat cooking oil in a medium frying pan, then add in the salmon, long green chili, Thai red chili, garlic, ginger, onion, and ground black pepper. | Next, add cane vinegar and water to the pan. Simmer on low heat for about 2 minutes then add in the coconut cream to the salmon. | Continue to simmer on low for another 20 minutes until the liquid has reduced to half and the salmon has cooked through. | Garnish with extra long green chili and Thai red chili, then serve as it is or with white rice!

coveo:metadata.title: Japanese Glass Noodle Salad
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Bean thread noodles 1 pack; Cucumber 1 piece; Carrot 1 piece; Rice vinegar 2 tbsp; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Sugar 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tbsp; Toasted sesame seeds 2 tsp; Chili flakes 1/4 tsp; Green onion 1 stalk
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Begin by boiling the bean thread noodles for 3-4 minutes, then drain and set aside. | Thinly slice the carrot diagonally, then cut into even matchsticks. | Next cut the cucumber in half, remove the seeds, then slice and cut into even matchsticks. | Make the dressing by combinging soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and sesame oil then stir until everything is combined. | Cut the cooked bean thread noodles, then add the cucumber, carrot, dressing, sesame seeds, chilli flakes and mix well so that everything is coated. | Mix everything again before serving, and garnish with the chopped green onion.

coveo:metadata.title: Thai Red Curry Shrimp
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Shrimp (peeled & deveined) 1 lbs; Olive oil 2 tbsp; Yellow onion 1 piece; Ginger (minced) 1 tbsp; Garlic (minced) 2-3 cloves; Bell pepper (red, orange, or green) 1 piece; Red curry paste 2 tbsp; Coconut milk 1 can; Salt 1 tsp; Ground black pepper 1 tsp; Lime 1-2 pieces; Cilantro 1 bunch
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large pan, heat olive oil on medium heat then add the onion slices and cook until the onions are softened. Next add ginger, garlic, salt, and black pepper and cook for 1 minute. | Then add the red curry paste, to the pan and stir together. Add the bell pepper and cook until softened, about 1-2 minutes. Afterwards add the coconut milk, then fill the can about halfway with water and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. | Season the peeled and deveined shrimp with salt and pepper then add it to the pot. Cover and simmer for another 1-2 minutes, or until the shrimp is cooked through. | Garnish with lime juice, lime wedge, and cilantro then serve over white rice!

coveo:metadata.title: Vietnamese Rice Noodle Salad with Chicken
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Boneless chicken thighs 1 lbs; Garlic 2 cloves + 3 cloves; Lime 1 piece + 1 piece; Fish sauce 2 tbsp + 1/4 cup; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Brown sugar 2 tbsp; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp; Rice vinegar 2 tbsp; White sugar 3 tbsp; Red Thai chili pepper 1 piece; Vermicelli rice noodles 1 pack; Bean sprouts 2 cups; Carrot 1 piece; Cucumber 1 piece; Mint 1 bunch; Cilantro 1 bunch; Romaine lettuce 1 bunch
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Make the marinade for the chicken by mixing together 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 lime (juiced), 2 tablespoons fish sauce, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 2 tablespoons brown sugar, and 1 tablespoon vegetable oil. | Add the boneless chicken thighs to the marinade and set aside for 10 minutes to 1 hour. | Prepare the Vermicelli rice noodles according to the package instructions, then drain and rinse with cold water. Set aside. | Make the Nuoc Cham sauce by combining 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 lime (juiced), 2 tablespoons rice vinegar, 1/4 cup fish sauce, 3 tablespoons sugar, 1 red Thai chili (or substitute 2 teaspoons Sriracha), 1/2 cup cold water and stir until the sugar has dissolved. | Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium high heat and sear the chicken for until cooked through. (around 3-5 minutes) | In a large bowl mix the rice noodles with bean sprouts, julienned carrots and cucumber, romaine lettuce, mint, and cilantro. Then slice the chicken thighs and add to the salad. Serve with Nuoc Cham sauce and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Sichuan Wontons in Chili Oil
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Lee Kum Kee Chiu Chow Style Chili Crisp Oil 1 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Chili Oil 1 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Seasoning Soy Sauce 1 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Selected Seasoned Aromatic Vinegar 2 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic 2 tsp; Sugar 1 tsp; Pork and shrimp wontons 6-8 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cook the pork wontons in boiling water, drain and place on a plate or bowl. | Combine all the seasoning ingredients and pour over the wontons. | Sprinkle with chopped green onions and serve.

coveo:metadata.title: Garlic Sesame Tofu
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Soy sauce 1 tbsp + 1/3 c; Extra firm tofu 1 pack; Cornstarch 1 tbsp + 1 tbsp; Panko bread crumbs 3 tbsp; Sesame oil 2 tsp; Rice vinegar 1 tsp; Honey 3 tbsp; Water 4 tbsp; Garlic 5 cloves; Vegetable oil 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Drain the excess liquid from the tofu using a paper towel. | Cut the tofu into 1 inch cubes and add to a large bowl. Toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp cornstarch, and 3 tbsp panko breadcrumbs until all pieces are evenly coated. | Add the tofu cubes to the baking tray and bake for 30-35 minutes at 400F until golden brown. | Make the sauce by adding garlic with 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a small saute pan for 2-3 minutes or until lightly browned. Then add in 1/3 c. soy sauce, 3 tbsp honey, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 2 tbsp of water, 2 tsp sesame oil, and mix well. | To thicken the sauce whisk together 1 tbsp cornstarch and another 2 tbsp of water in a bowl and add to the mixture. Heat over low heat for 3-5 minutes or until the sauce bubbles and thickens. | When the tofu is crispy, toss the tofu cubes with the sauce. Serve over rice with steamed broccoli or enjoy as is!

coveo:metadata.title: Thai Green Papaya Salad
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Green papaya (julienned) 1 1/2 c; Garlic 2 cloves; Thai chili pepper 2-4 pieces; Palm sugar 1 1/2 tbsp; Long beans 2-3 pieces; Roasted peanuts 3 tbsp; Dried shrimp 1 tbsp; Lime 1 piece; Tamarind paste 2 tsp; Fish sauce 1 1/2 tbsp; Grape tomato 1/2 c
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Start by peeling and julienning the green papaya, then soak the papaya in cold water. Drain the soaked papaya well and place in a bowl lined with 2 layers of paper towel to absorb excess water. | Using a mortar and pestle, pound garlic and chilies until there are no more big chunks (pieces of chili skin are fine). | Add palm sugar and mash with the pestle until it turns into a wet paste and all the chunks are dissolved. | Add long beans and pound just until they're broken. | Add dried shrimp and peanuts and pound to break the peanuts up slightly. | Add fish sauce, tamarind paste, and lime juice then stir. | Add the dressing and sliced grape tomatoes to the shredded papaya. | Mix everything together until well incorporated, and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Udon Noodle Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Water 2 cups; Udon noodles 1 pack; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Sugar 1/4 tsp; Mirin 1 tbsp; Dashi powder 1/2 tbsp; Tsuyu 1 Tbsp; Green onion 1 stalk; Furikake 1/8 tsp; Tofu skins (or bean curd) 1-2 sheets
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a medium pan add water, instant dashi powder, soy sauce, sugar, mirin, and tsuyu. Then bring to a simmer over medium heat for about 2-3 minutes. | Once the broth has begun simmering add the udon noodles directly into the hot broth. Use a spoon to gently loosen the noodles and cook for 1-3 minutes until the noodles have softened. | Transfer to a large bowl, then garnish with green onion, furikake seaweed seasoning, fried bean curd, and narutomaki (fishcake). Serve and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Quick Spicy Korean Noodle Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken broth 4 cups; Kimchi 2/3 cups; Kimchi juice (from the container) 2 tbsp; Gochujang 3 tbsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Ginger 1 piece; Garlic 1-2 cloves; Egg noodles 8 oz; Choi sum 4 pieces; Carrot 1 piece; Sesame oil 2 1/2 tsp; Green onion 1 stalk
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large stock pot add in 4 cups chicken broth, 2/3 cup cabbage kimchi, 2 tbsp kimchi juice, 3 tbsp gochujang, 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine, 2 slices ginger, and 2 garlic gloves. Then cook on mediun heat for about 10 minutes until the broth begins to simmer. | Next, add choi sum stems and carrot to the broth, then simmer for another 2 minutes. When the stems and carrot soften a bit add choi sum leaves and sesame oil, then simmer for 1 minute. | Cook the egg noodles in boiling water according to the packet directions. Then drain the noodles and quickly rinse it under tap water to remove extra starch. (Make sure to shake off excess water well.) | Begin plating the noodles in bowls and top with the sauteed vegetables. Pick the garlic and ginger out of the broth, then broth pour over the noodles and vegetables. Garnish with green onions and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Easy Curry Laksa Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Vegetable oil 2 tbsp; Thai red curry paste 2 tbsp; Curry powder 2 tbsp; Boneless chicken thighs 6 oz; Coconut milk 1 can (13.5 fl oz); Chicken stock 4 c; Peel & deveined shrimp 14 oz; Fish ball 6 oz; Tofu puff 6-8 pieces; Fish sauce 2 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp; Egg noodles (can use Vermicelli or Wheat noodles) 1/2 lbs; Bean sprouts 1/2 c; Egg 1-2 pieces; Cilantro 1 bunch
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium-high heat then add the curry paste and cook, stirring, for a minute or until fragrant. Stir in the curry powder, then add the chicken thighs and cook for 1-2 minutes. Next add coconut milk and chicken stock to the mixture. Bring the soup to a low boil and simmer for about 5 minutes. | Now add the shrimp, fish balls, and tofu puffs to the soup. Simmer for a another few minutes or until the shrimp is fully cooked. | Add fish sauce and sugar. (Season to taste, add extra if needed.) Ladle your laksa soup broth over the noodles and garnish with bean sprouts, a boiled egg and cilantro.

coveo:metadata.title: Thai Coconut Squash Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Vegetable oil 2 tbsp; Thai red curry paste 3 tbsp; Garlic 1-2 cloves; Yellow onion 1 piece; Coconut milk 1 can (13.5 fl oz); Chicken stock 2 1/2 c; Butternut squash (can use pumpkin or kabocha) 1 piece; Fish sauce 1 tbsp; Cilantro 1 bunch; Fried shallots 1 tsp; Red Thai chili 1 piece; Roti 1 piece
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium high heat. Add onion and garlic, cook for 2 minutes until soft. | Add curry paste and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant. | Add squash and stir to coat in the flavors for around 2 minutes. | Set aside 1/4 cup coconut milk for garnish. Add stock, remaining coconut milk and fish sauce. Bring to simmer then reduce heat to medium and simmer for 8 minutes until the pumpkin is soft. | Next, blend the soup mixture until everything is smooth and well incorporated. | Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with fried shallots, red Thai chili slices, and cilantro. Serve with roti bread and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Tteokbokki with Ramen
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken stock 3 cups; Korean rice cakes (Tteok) 1 cup; Instant ramen 1 pack; Fish cake (cut in small pieces) 2 sheets; Green onion 3 stalks; Shredded cabbage 1 cup; Boiled eggs 1-2 pieces; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Sugar 1 1/2 tbsp; Chili flakes 1 tbsp; Minced garlic 2 cloves; Honey 2 tbsp; Gochujang 3 tbsp; Sesame seeds 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a small bowl mix together the soy sauce, sugar, garlic, chili flakes, Gochujang paste, and honey. | Add chicken stock to a large pot, then when it begins to simmer add in the Gochujang sauce and the rice cakes (Tteok). Boil for about 7 minutes. | Next, add in the fish cakes, ramen, shredded cabbage, and green onions. Stir well to make sure everything is covered in the sauce. | Cook the mixture until the ramen noodles soften, then add in hard boiled eggs. | When the ramen noodles have fully cooked, serve in a bowl and garnish with extra green onions and sesame seeds. Enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Kabocha with Pork and Mushrooms
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Kabocha squash 1 piece; Green onions 2 stalks; Minced garlic 2 tsp; Enoki mushrooms 1 pack; Avocado oil 2 tbsp; Salt 1 tsp; Ground pork 1/2 lbs; Chicken broth 2 tbsp; Sesame oil 3 tsp; Shirataki noodles 1 pack; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Rice vinegar 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat a large skillet, then add avocado oil, garlic, and white parts of green onion, and salt on medium heat until fragrant. | Next add ground pork to the skillet and season with salt and use a wooden spoon to break up the meat. Cook through until it’s no longer pink. | Add squash, mushrooms, sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice vinegar then lower the heat. Stir thoroughly, then add 2 tbsp stock and cover with a lid to simmer until the squash is tender. | Drain and rinse the shirataki noodles according to the package instructions. Then add the noodles to the pan and stir to coat the noodles with the meat and sauce. | Turn off the heat. Drizzle with more sesame oil and garnish with more green green onions, then enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Pumpkin Omelet
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Kabocha 1 piece; Green onions 2 stalks; Eggs 3 pieces; Black pepper 1/2 tsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Salt 1/2 tsp; Oil 2 tbsp; Sesame seeds 1/2 tsp; Soy Sauce (for dipping) 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, pepper, and cooking wine. Mix in the pumpkin and chives, then set aside. | Heat oil in a 10-inch non-stick pan over medium-low, tilting to coat evenly. Once warm, pour in the mixture and spread it out with a spatula. | Cover and cook for 5 minutes, then flip with a plate and cook uncovered for another 4-5 minutes. | Top with toasted sesame seeds and serve with soy sauce for dipping.

coveo:metadata.title: Mung Bean Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Dried mung beans 1 cup; Dried lily bulb 1/3 cup; Glutinous rice 2 tbsp; Sago 1/2 cup; Sugar 1 tbsp; Water 8 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: First, rinse the mung beans, dried lily bulb pieces, and sweet rice under cold water. Then add them to a large bowl and cover with water. Soak for 8 hours or overnight. | Drain the beans, lily bulb, and rice, then transfer to a large pot with 8 cups of water. Next bring it to a boil, cover, and simmer for about 1 hour, or until the beans are very soft. | Add the sago to the soup after it’s been cooking for about 40 minutes. It will take about 20 minutes for the sago to cook (it will be translucent). | Now add in your sugar to taste. You can also refrain from adding sugar at this point, and each person can sweeten their individual bowls as they like.

coveo:metadata.title: Asian Twist Charcuterie Board
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Roasted chestnuts 1 cup; Homemade pickles 1 cup; Chinese pickled cucumbers 1 cup; Brie cheese 1 wedge; Sesame mochi 4-6 pieces; Pepperjack cheese 4-6 pieces; Gyoza 4-6 pieces; Black pepper scone 2-4 pieces; Wasabi peas 1 cup; Grapes 1 cup; Egg roll cookies 6-8 pieces; Sugar snap peas 1/2 cup; Dried apricot 1/2 cup; Chips 1 cup; Mandarins 4-6 pieces; Pineapple 4-6 pieces; Papaya 1 piece
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Arrange smaller bites and sauces in small bowls, then group the other ingredients on a large wooden cutting board or platter to serve.

coveo:metadata.title: Sichuan Green Beans
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Green beans 1 lbs; Avocado oil 2 tbsp; Ground pork 1/4 lbs; Sichuan peppercorn 1/4 tsp; Dried chili pepper 2-4 pieces; Garlic 3 cloves; Ginger 1 tsp; Shaoxing wine 2 tbsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp; Salt 1/2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add green beans and a pinch of salt, stirring to coat with oil. Spread beans to minimize overlap and flip every 15 seconds. Cook 6-8 minutes until some beans are browned and softened. For a softer texture, cook 10-12 minutes until charred, lowering heat if needed. Transfer beans to a plate. | Add 1 tablespoon oil, ground pork, and ground Sichuan peppercorns to the skillet. Cook through, breaking the pork into small pieces. | Next, add dried chili pepper, garlic, and ginger. Stir briefly, then return green beans to the skillet. Add Shaoxing wine, soy sauce, and sugar, then cook until sauce is mostly absorbed (1-2 minutes). | Lower heat, taste, and adjust salt if needed. Serve hot as a side or main dish, and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese 5 Spice Cranberry Sauce
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Water 1 cup; Port Wine 2/3 cup; Brown sugar 3/4 cup; Dried cherries 1/3 cup; Fresh or frozen cranberries 12 oz; Orange juice 1-2 tbsp; Orange zest 1-2 tbsp; Red wine vinegar 1 tbsp; Chinese 5 spice powder 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine water, brown sugar, port, and dried cherries over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. | Increase to high heat, add fresh or frozen cranberries, and bring to a boil. Reduce to medium heat and simmer for 10 minutes until some cranberries pop and the liquid reduces slightly. Lower the heat if it boils too vigorously. Then stir in orange juice, zest, vinegar, and Chinese 5 spice. If you like a more jammy cranberry sauce continue to cook the cranberries on low for an additional 5 to 10 minutes. | Transfer to a heat-safe container, let cool, and refrigerate until ready to use!

coveo:metadata.title: Steamed Eggs
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Eggs 3 pieces; Water 1.5 cups; Chicken broth 1.5 cups; Salt 1 tsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Green onion 2 stalks
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Crack 3 eggs into a liquid measuring cup and note the volume. Transfer to a bowl, add salt, and beat for 1 minute. Measure equal volumes of water and broth, add to the eggs, and whisk in sesame oil until combined. | Strain the mixture into a heatproof dish and place inside a steamer. | Boil water in a steamer. Steam the eggs over high heat for 3 minutes, then turn off the heat and leave covered for 14 minutes. | Garnish with scallions and more sesame oil, then serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Tomato Tofu
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Avocado oil 2 tbsp; Green onion 1 bunch; Tomato 1 lbs; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Water 1/2 c; Sugar 1 tbsp; Salt 1/2 tsp; Oyster sauce 1 tbsp; Soy sauce 2 tsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; White pepper 1/2 tsp; Cornstarch 2 tsp; Soft tofu 1-2 packs
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat a stainless steel wok or skillet over high heat. Add neutral oil and the white parts of the scallions. Stir for 15 seconds, then add the tomatoes. | Stir-fry for 2 minutes, then add Shaoxing wine around the pan’s edge. | Add water (or stock), sugar, salt, vegetarian oyster sauce, light soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper. Simmer for 1 minute until tomatoes soften. | Prepare a cornstarch slurry and stir it into the sauce to thicken. Add tofu, gently mixing to avoid breaking it. | Reduce heat to medium, cover, and cook for 2 minutes until the tomatoes and sauce are thickened. Adjust with more slurry if needed. | Uncover, stir in scallions, and season with salt and sugar to taste. Serve with jasmine rice and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Microwave Hot Pot
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Enoki mushrooms 100g; Carrot 1/2 piece; Cherry tomatoes 5-6 pieces; Bok choy (or Asian greens of choice) 1 cup; Cabbage 200g; Thinly sliced beef 200g; Garlic 1 tsp; Ginger 1 tsp; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Dashi or miso stock 500ml
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Assemble the Hotpot: Layer enoki mushrooms, carrot slices, cherry tomatoes, green vegetables, cabbage, and shabu shabu beef in a large microwave-safe container. | Mix garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and dashi or miso stock. Pour it over the ingredients. | Cover with a microwave-safe lid or plate and microwave on high for 6-8 minutes until the beef is cooked and veggies are tender. | Stir, serve, and enjoy this easy hotpot hack straight from your microwave!

coveo:metadata.title: Chili Oil Noodles
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Garlic 2 cloves; Ginger 1/2 tbsp; Green onions 2-3 stalks; Chili flakes 1/2 - 1 tbsp; Sesame seeds 1/2 tbsp; Sugar 1/2 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Chinese black vinegar 1 tsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; MSG (optional) 1/4 tsp; neutral oil 2 tbsp; Knife cut noodles 5-6 oz
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl, combine garlic, ginger, green onion, chili flakes, sesame seeds, and sugar. | Heat 2 tbsp of neutral oil (e.g., avocado oil) to 375°F, then carefully pour it over the chili flakes and aromatics. Stir well. | Add sesame oil, Chinese black vinegar, soy sauce, MSG, and salt to taste. | Cook noodles to your liking, mix them with the sauce, and add a bit of noodle water if needed to adjust the consistency. Enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Sweet and Sour Pork
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pork shoulder butt 1/2 lbs; Red bell pepper 1 piece; Yellow bell pepper 1 piece; Green bell pepper 1 piece; Red onion 1 piece; Pineapple 1/4 cup; Corn oil1-2 cups; Garlic salt 1 tsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Egg 1 piece; Flour 2 tbsp; Cornstarch 4 tbsp; Ketchup 3 tbsp; Brown sugar 3 tbsp; Vinegar 2 tbsp; Salt 1/4 tsp; Water 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut the pork into 1/4 to 1/2 inch pieces, slicing both parallel and perpendicular to the grain. Mix with garlic salt and soy sauce, massaging for 1 minute. Crack and beat the egg, mix with the pork for 30 seconds. Let the pork marinate while preparing the other ingredients. | Cut bell peppers, red onions, and pineapple into bite-sized pieces. | Make the sauce by mixing ketchup, brown sugar, vinegar, cornstarch, salt, and water until smooth. | Coat the pork with flour, massaging for 1-2 minutes. Add cornstarch to a plate and coat the pork pieces. | Heat oil in a wok to 300-350°F (149-176°C). Fry the pork for 7-8 minutes until browned. For a second fry, heat the oil to 400°F (204°C) and fry for 1-2 minutes. When the pork has cooked, drain on paper towel and set aside. | Stir-fry the vegetables for 30 seconds, then add the pineapple and cook for another 30 seconds. Remove from the wok. Reduce the sauce in the wok for 1-2 minutes. | Add everything back into the wok, toss with the sauce for 1-2 minutes. Serve immediately for crunchiness!

coveo:metadata.title: Mala Hot Pot
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Water 4-6 cups; Mala Hot Pot Sauce (LKK SOUP BASE SC STYLE HOT & SPICY (MALA) 1 pack; Garlic 2 cloves; Green Onion 2-4 stalks; Soy Sauce (LKK) 2-4 tbsp; Ramen Noodles (WU-MU RAMEN NOODLE ORIGINAL) 1 pack; Canned Straw Mushrooms (ASN/TAS STRAW MUSHROOM BROKEN) 1 can; Fish Tofu (J/JANE FZ FISH TOFU) 1 cup; Milkfish Ball (J/JANE FZ MILKFISH BALL) 1 cup; Canned Baby Corn (Yifa) 1 can; Quail Eggs (ASN/TAS) 1 can; Regular Corn 1 pieces; Leafy Greens 1/2 cup; Napa Cabbage 1/2 cup; Carrots 1/2 piece; Mushrooms 1/4 cup; Croissant Ring Roll (EVERBEST CROISSANT RING ROLL) 3 pieces; Chili Sauce (AGV) 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large pot add the garlic, chopped green onion, water, hot pot seasoning, and soy sauce. Stir it well and bring it to a low boil. | When the mixture has begun to boil add the noodles, canned straw mushrooms, fish tofu, fish balls, baby corn, quail eggs, corn, leafy greens, cabbage, carrot slices, fresh mushrooms, and croissant ring rolls. | Cover the pot with a lid and prepare the dipping sauce. In a small dish add about 2 tablespoons of chili sauce and set aside for later. | When the ingredients have cooked to your liking, turn off the heat. Dip your food into the chili sauce and enjoy your Mala Hot Pot!

coveo:metadata.title: Longevity Noodles
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Yi Mein noodles 1/2 lbs; Cooking oil 2 tbsp; Garlic 3-4 cloves; Onion 1 piece; Carrot 1 piece; Shitake mushrooms 4-5 pieces; Chicken broth 1 cup; Oyster sauce 2 tbsp; Light soy sauce 1 tbsp; Dark soy sauce 1/2 tbsp; Sugar 1/2 tsp; Ground white pepper 1/2 tsp; Salt 1/4 tsp; Green onions 4-5 stalks; Sesame oil 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Boil plenty of water and cook the noodles until al dente. Drain and set aside. | Heat a wok until very hot, then add oil and swirl to coat the surface. Add garlic and onion, cooking until fragrant. Stir in carrots and mushrooms, and cook for 1 minute. | Pour in the stock, followed by oyster sauce, soy sauces, sugar, and white pepper. Bring to a boil and taste. Adjust salt if needed. | Add the noodles to the wok, gently tossing to coat with the liquid. Simmer on low heat until most of the liquid is absorbed. | Stir in the chives and sesame oil, tossing until the chives wilt. Serve immediately and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chimei Mantou
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: CHIMEI FZ WHOLE WHEAT ALMOND MANTOU 5PC 1 pack; Hoisin sauce 4-6 tbsp; Roasted beef shank 1 lbs; Pickled cucumbers 1 cup; Cilantro 1 bunch; CHIMEI FZ BROWN SUGAR SLICE ROLL 6PC 1 pack; Sweetened condensed milk 1 can
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Place frozen mantou onto steamer and steam for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked. | Place the Chimei Brown Sugar Slice Rolls on a plate, and serve with one can of sweetened condensed milk for dipping. | Slice the Chimei Whole Wheat Almond Mantou in half after steaming. | Open the mantou, and spread hoisin sauce on each side. Then fill with roasted beef shank, pickled cucumbers, and cilantro. | Serve and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Synear Rice Balls
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Water 4-6 cups; Rock sugar 3-4 pieces; SYNEAR RICE BALL PURPLE CHESTNUT 1 pack; SYNEAR AUTHENTIC RICE BALL SESAME 1 pack; SYNEAR MINI RICE BALL SESAME/PEANUT/STRW 1 pack
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large saucepan add water and rock sugar, then bring to a low boil until the rock sugar has dissolved. | After the rock sugar has dissovled in the water, add in the frozen Synear Tan Yuan to the saucepan. | Boil the Synear Tan Yuan for about 4 minutes. | Seve the Tan Yuan in a bowl with the rock sugar water and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Buddha's Delight (Lo Hon Zai)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Dried shitake mushroom 3-4 pieces; Dried lily flower 1/4 cup; Wood ear fungus (or black fungus) 1/8 cup; Mung bean vermicelli 1/2 oz; Bean curd sheet 1 sheet; Cabbage 1/2 cup; Bamboo shoots 1/4 cup; Carrot 1 piece; Baby sweet corn 1/4 cup; Fatt choy (black moss) 2 tsp; Sugar 2 tbsp; White fermented bean curd 1/2 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Tie knots in lily flowers, then cut the carrots, bamboo shoots, mushrooms and cabbage. | Soak dry ingredients (lily flowers, wood ear fungus, shiitake mushrooms, black moss) until soft. Reserve mushroom water. | Cut the bean curd sheet into smaller pieces, deep-fry for 15-20 seconds, then drain. | Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok and stir-fry the cabbage until slightly softened. | Add shiitake mushrooms, lily flowers, wood ear fungus, black moss, carrots, fermented bean curd, sugar, mushroom soaking water, and extra water. Braise for 2 minutes. | Break the fried bean curd into smaller pieces and lay them on top. | Place mung bean vermicelli over the bean curd, cover, and braise for 5 minutes until soft, adding water if needed. | Add bamboo shoots and baby sweet corn. Continue braising until the vegetables are tender and the sauce thickens. | Mix everything well and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Peanut Cookies
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Creamy peanut butter 3/4 cups; Peanut oil 3 tbsp; Flour 1 3/4 cups; Powdered sugar 4/7 cups; Salt 1/4 tsp; Egg 1 piece; Water 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine peanut butter, sugar, flour, and salt. Use a wooden spoon to stir until evenly mixed. Gradually add half of the cooking oil, mixing as you go rather than pouring it all in at once. Continue adding the remaining oil slowly, incorporating it with your hands until a cohesive dough forms. If the dough feels too dry, add more oil, one teaspoon at a time, until it holds together without crumbling. | Roll the dough into 1-tablespoon-sized balls and arrange them on a parchment-lined cookie sheet, spacing them about 1/4 inch apart. | Use a fork to gently flatten the dough ball while creating a crisscross pattern, or simply press a half piece of peanut on top of the cookie dough. | Prepare the egg wash by whisking together one beaten egg and one teaspoon of water. Lightly brush the cookies with the egg wash, then bake at 350°F on the middle rack for 15–18 minutes. Rotate the pan halfway through baking to ensure even browning. The cookies should turn golden brown. If making larger cookies, extend the baking time by a few minutes as needed. | Remove from the oven and let them rest for about 15 minutes before transferring them to a cookie rack to cool completely. Once fully cooled, serve and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Dua Mon (Vietnamese Pickled Daikon)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Daikon 1 piece (about 2 lbs); Sea salt 1 tbsp; Fish sauce 1/2 cup; Brown sugar 1/2 cup (packed); Water 1/4 cup; Garlic 2-3 pieces; Red chili pepper 1-2 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut the daikon into 4-inch pieces, then slice each piece into small wedges. Place the cut daikon in a large mixing bowl, add salt, and toss to combine. Let the mixture sit for 2 hours to allow the water to pool at the bottom. Afterward, rinse the daikon and squeeze out the excess salt in batches. | Arrange the daikon on a wire rack set over a baking sheet and preheat the oven to its lowest setting, usually around 170°F. Allow it to dry for 1-2 hours, or longer if necessary. | In a small saucepan, combine the fish sauce, brown sugar, and water. Heat the mixture over medium heat until it begins to bubble, then remove from the heat. Allow it to cool to room temperature. | Transfer the dried daikon to a jar with a lid and add the sliced garlic and chili peppers. | Pour the brine into the jar and secure with a lid. Let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours, then transfer it to the fridge. It will be ready to eat after 1 day, but the flavor will improve with time. When stored in the fridge, it can last for up to 8 months.

coveo:metadata.title: Tteokguk (Korean Rice Cake Soup)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Sliced rice cakes (tteok) 1 lbs; Water 5-7 cups; Beef stew meat (or flank steak/brisket) 1/2 lbs; Garlic 2-4 cloves; Green onion 2-3 stalks; Vegetable oil 3 tsp; Eggs 2 pieces; Fish sauce 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 1 tsp; Black pepper 1/2 tsp; Toasted seaweed paper (nori) 1 sheet; Red pepper 1 piece; Salt 2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Start preparing the ingredients by cutting the beef, garlic, green onions, and red pepper. Then separate the egg yolks from whites, and season with salt. | In a medium pan, cook the egg yolks then slice into strips. | While the burner is on roast the seaweed paper sheet (nori) and set aside. | In a large pot begin boiling about 5-7 cups of water at medium heat. When the water is at a rapid boil add in the garlic, beef, sliced rice cakes (tteok), fish sauce, and salt. | Add in the egg whites to the soup and continue to stir while pouring to create ribbons of egg whites. Then add sesame oil, black pepper, and green onions. | Remove from heat and serve the rice cake soup into bowls. Garnish with yellow egg strips, crushed seaweed, red pepper and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Soy Braised Tilapia
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Tilapia (filets) 1 lbs; Vegetable oil 1 tsp; Ginger 1 tbsp; Garlic 1 tbsp; Light sodium soy sauce 1/4 cup; Rice vinegar 1/4 cup; Green onions 1/4 cup; Cilantro 1/4 cup; Crushed chili 1/2 tsp; Water 1/4 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat oil in a deep skillet over medium heat. Add ginger and garlic, sautéing for 30 to 60 seconds until fragrant. | Place the fish fillets on the hot oil and fry for about 1 minute. Then add soy sauce, water, scallions, cilantro, crushed chili, and vinegar to the skillet. Bring everything to a boil, then cover and let it cook for 3-4 minutes. | Once done, remove from heat and transfer to a plate, drizzling the flavorful sauce over the fish. Garnish with extra green onions and cilantro. Enjoy over white rice!

coveo:metadata.title: Garlic Shrimp Pasta
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: 99 Ranch Organic Brown Rice Pasta 1 pack; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp & 1/4 cup; Peeled and deveined shrimps 1 lbs; Garlic 3-4 cloves; 99 Ranch chilli flakes 1 tsp; 99 Ranch soy sauce 2 tbsp; Oyster sauce 2 tbsp; Green onions 3 stalks; Ginger 1 piece; Salt 1 tsp; Sesame oil 2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Use a mortar and pestle to mash the spring onion, ginger, and salt into a rough paste. Next heat vegetable oil and sesame oil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat and pour it over the spring onion mixture. Stir and set aside. | Cook the pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Make sure to save 1 cup of pasta water. | Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add garlic, shrimp, and chili flakes. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the shrimp turn pink. Stir in soy sauce and oyster sauce, simmer briefly, then turn off the heat. | Drain pasta and add it to the pan. Turn heat to high and toss for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens. Add pasta water if needed. | Stir in the spring onion sauce, mix well, and serve!

coveo:metadata.title: Lee Kum Kee Kimchi Fried Rice
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Cooking oil 1 tbsp; Chopped onion 2 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic 1 tbsp; Cooked rice 1 bowl; Kimchi (cut to pieces) 1 cup; Eggs 2 pieces; Chopped green onions 1 tbsp; Shredded seaweed as desired; Cooked white sesame 1 tsp; Lee Kum Kee Panda Brand Oyster Flavored Sauce 2 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Premium Pure Sesame Oil 1 tbsp; Kimchi juice 1/3 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a heated wok, add cooking oil and sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until it is a little charred. Add in cooked rice and mix well (need to press down to break the rice clump). Then add in kimchi and kimchi juice to stir fry. | Add in seasoning and stir fry well before serving in a plate. | In addition, crack the eggs in to boiling water and cook slowly for 3 minutes to make poached eggs. | Finally, sprinkle with chopped green onion, shredded seaweed and cooked white sesame to complete. | Tips: adding oyster sauce to this dish can help boost the flavor.

coveo:metadata.title: Braised Korean Short Ribs (Galbijjim)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Beef short ribs 4-5 lbs; Yellow onion 1 piece; Ginger 5-6 slices; Garlic 5-6 cloves; Turnips (can use daikon or Chinese radish) 8 pieces; Shimeji mushrooms (or Shitake mushrooms) 1 pack; Carrots 3 pieces; HANASIA KOREAN BBQ SAUCE KALBI 1 bottle; Water 8 cups & 4 cups; Dried jujubes (optional) 2-3 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Boil the beef short ribs in 8 cups of water for 10 minutes, then drain and rinse to clean the meat. | Transfer the cleaned short ribs to a large pot and add 1 bottle of Hanasia Korean BBQ sauce (kalbi) along with 4 cups of water, ensuring the meat is fully submerged. Bring to a boil over medium heat and cook for 30-45 minutes, or longer for more tender ribs. | Meanwhile, peel and slice the onion, garlic, ginger, turnips, mushrooms, and carrots. Add all the vegetables except the onions to the pot, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes. | Uncover, then add the onions and optional dried jujubes. Cook for another 10 minutes, or until the sauce thickens. | Serve over white rice and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Korean Gimbap
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Seaweed paper 5 sheets; Cooked rice 4 cups; Beef skirt steak (or tenderloin, or ground beef) 1/2 lbs; Carrot 1-2 pieces; Yellow pickled radish 4-5 pieces; Spinach 1 bunch; Eggs 2-3 pieces; Garlic 3 cloves; Soy sauce 2 tsp; Sugar 1 tbsp & 1 tsp; Salt 1 tsp; Sesame oil 2 tbsp; Vegetable oil (to cook the egg) 1 tsp; Black pepper 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Place freshly cooked rice in a large, shallow bowl. Gently mix in salt and sesame oil using a rice scoop or wooden spoon. Set aside. | Blanch spinach, then mix in minced garlic, salt, and sesame oil in a bowl. | Cut carrot into matchsticks, mix with salt, and let it sit for 5-10 minutes. Heat a pan with a few drops of vegetable oil. Squeeze out excess water from the carrot, then sauté for 1 minute. Place next to the spinach. | Trim fat from skirt steaks and slice into ¼ inch wide strips. Mix with soy sauce, minced garlic, black pepper, 1 tablespoon + 1 teaspoon brown sugar, and sesame oil. | Heat a pan over medium-high and cook the marinated beef, stirring until fully cooked. Set aside. | Beat 3 eggs with ¼ teaspoon salt. Heat a 10-12 inch non-stick pan with a few drops of oil. Wipe off excess oil with a paper towel, leaving a thin layer. Pour egg mixture into the pan, spreading it into a large circle. | Once the bottom is cooked, flip the egg and remove from heat. Let it cook in the pan for 5 minutes without browning. Slice into ½ inch strips and set aside with spinach. | To roll the gimbap, place a sheet of gim on a bamboo mat with the shiny side down. Spread about ¾ cup of rice, leaving 2 inches uncovered at the top. | Add beef, carrot, pickled radish, egg strips, and spinach in the center. Roll the mat tightly, pressing as you go. | Brush with sesame oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Slice into ¼ inch pieces with a sharp knife, wiping the knife clean between cuts. Serve and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Sesame Soba Noodles
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Soy sauce 1/4 cup; Sesame oil 3 tbsp; Rice vinegar 2 tbsp; Brown sugar 1 tbsp; Ginger 1 tsp; Black pepper 1 tsp; Soba noodles (buckwheat noodles) 1 pack; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp; Green onion 3 stalks; Sesame seeds 2 tbsp; Seaweed flakes 1/2 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine the soy sauce, sesame oil, vinegar, brown sugar, ginger, and pepper. Stir well and set aside. | Cook the buckwheat noodles according to the package instructions. | Once cooked, rinse them under cold running water until fully cooled. Drain thoroughly and set aside. | Heat oil in a wok or skillet over medium heat. Add half of the green onions and sauté for about 30 seconds. | Pour in the prepared sauce and bring to a boil. Let it simmer and thicken slightly, about 15 seconds. | Add the noodles and the remaining green onions to the pan. Toss everything together until evenly coated. | Add in sesame seeds and crumbled seaweed flakes, then mix well. | Garnish with more green onion, sesame seeds, crumbled seaweed flakes and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Lee Kum Kee Boiled Seafood Platter
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Shell-on shrimp 10 oz; Mussels 8 pieces; Squid rings 6 oz; Crab sticks 4 pieces; Fresh scallops 6 oz; Vegetable oil 4 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic 3 tbsp; Minced millet pepper 2 tbsp; Chopped scallion (for garnish) 2 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Liquid Seasoning 6 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil 1 tbsp
