coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cook the seafood in separate batches in boiling water, draining each well and placing onto a plate. | Heat the oil in a pot and quickly stir-fry the Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and millet pepper until fragrant. | Drizzle the mixture over the cooked seafood. | Scatter chopped scallion over the dish, and drizzle the seafood with Lee Kum Kee Liquid Seasoning and Lee Kum Kee Pure Sesame Oil to serve.

coveo:metadata.title: Japanese Beef Curry
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Vegetable oil 1 tsp; Cubed beef (can use chuck roast or stew meat) 1/2-1 lbs; Onion 1 piece; Carrot 1-2 pieces; Potato 1 piece; Garlic 2 cloves; Water 2 cups; Japanese curry cube 1 cube
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat and sear beef for 2 minutes until browned, then remove and set aside. | Add onions and garlic then cook for about 8 minutes until soft and caramelized. | Stir in cut carrots and potatoes, and cook for 1 more minute. | Return beef to the pot and add 2 cups of water. | Stir, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. | Stir in Japanese curry roux cube until fully dissolved, then bring back to medium-low heat and simmer for another 15 minutes until thickened. | Enjoy over rice!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Tea Eggs
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Eggs 1 dozen; Light soy sauce 4 tbsp; Dark soy sauce 2 tbsp; Bay leaves 2-3 pieces; Sichuan peppercorns 1 tsp; Star anise 1-2 pieces; Cinnamon stick 1 piece; Sugar 2 tsp; Salt 1 tsp; Black tea 2 tea bags; Water 3 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Mix all marinade ingredients in a small pot. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Let it cool completely, then remove the tea bags. | Boil enough water to cover your eggs. Lower the heat and gently add the eggs using a spoon. (Soft-boiled: 5 minutes, Medium: 7 minutes, or Hard-boiled: 10 minutes) | Transfer eggs to a bowl of ice water for 2–3 minutes, or rinse with cold tap water until cool. Then gently crack the shells with a spoon (don’t break the eggs), or peel them if you prefer. | Transfer the eggs to a container, then pour in the marinade along with the dry ingredients and marinate overnight for peeled eggs, or 24 hours for cracked “marble” eggs. | Peel the eggs and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chicken Satay
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Bonless chicken thighs 1 lbs; Coconut milk 1 can; Curry powder 1 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp + 1/4 cup; Red curry paste 2 tsp + 2 tbsp; Salt 1 tsp + 1 tsp; Apple cider vinegar 2 tbsp; Dark soy sauce 2 tsp; Peanut butter 1/2 cup; Water 1/2 cup; Crushed peanuts 1 tbsp; Lime 2 pieces; Cilantro 1 bunch; Thai chili 2 pieces; Bamboo skewers 1 pack
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Make the marinade by mixing together 1 tbsp curry powder, 1 tsp sugar, 2 tsp red curry paste, and 1 tsp salt. | Now mix the chicken pieces with the marinade and ¼ cup coconut milk. Let sit for at least 20 minutes or overnight. | Place about 4–5 chicken pieces onto each skewer. | Heat oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high and cook skewers in batches, about 3 minutes per side until golden. | In a saucepan, combine remaining coconut milk with 2 tbsp red curry paste, 1/2 cup peanut butter, 1/4 cup sugar, 2 tsp dark soy sauce, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp cider vinegar, and 1/2 cup water. | Stir and simmer over medium-low heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. | Pour some sauce into a bowl and sprinkle with crushed peanuts. Pile satay skewers onto a platter, sprinkle with remaining peanuts, coriander and chilli. | Serve with sauce on the side for dipping and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Hawaiian Loco Moco
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Eggs 1-2 pieces; Unsalted butter 2 tbsp; Onion 1 piece; Mushrooms (sliced) 1 pack; Cooked rice 2 cups; Green onions 3 stalks; Ground beef 1 1/2 lbs; Worcestershire sauce 1 tbsp + 1 tsp; Seasoning salt 1 tsp; Garlic powder 1 tsp; Black pepper 1/2 tsp; Beef stock 1 1/2 cup; Soy sauce 4 tsp; Ketchup 2 tsp; Cornstarch 5 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a large bowl, mix ground beef, Worcestershire sauce, seasoning salt, garlic powder, and black pepper. Shape into thick, round patties (about 1 inch thick), smoothing the edges. | Heat a pan over medium heat and cook patties for 3–5 minutes on each side, or until fully cooked. Set cooked patties on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess grease. | In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp butter and sauté onions for 1 minute, then add mushrooms for another 1–2 minutes. | Whisk together beef stock, shoyu, Worcestershire sauce, ketchup, and cornstarch until smooth. Stir until it thickens and starts to simmer. | Melt a little butter in a pan, crack in the egg, and cook sunny-side up (or to your preference). Set aside. | Scoop hot rice onto a plate, add a hamburger patty, pour over the gravy, and top with the egg. | Garnish with green onions and serve hot!

coveo:metadata.title: Salmon Fillet with Mushroom
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: White Mushrooms 250g; Salmon Fillet 2 pieces; Asparagus Spears 4-6 pieces; Onion (chopped) 20g; Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic 10g; Lee Kum Kee Supreme Authentic First Draw Soy Sauce 2 tbsp; Lee Kum Kee Coarse Ground Black Peppercorns A pinch; Water 3 tbsp; Sugar 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Sauté Lee Kum Kee Minced Garlic and chopped onions until fragrant. Add Lee Kum Kee Supreme Authentic First Draw Soy Sauce, water, and sugar. Bring to a boil and mix well. Remove from heat and set the sauce aside. | Blanch the asparagus until tender, then drain and arrange on a serving plate. | Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Pan-fry the white mushrooms until golden brown. Add 2 tablespoons of the prepared sauce, stir well, and remove from the pan. | Heat oil in the pan again and add the salmon fillets. Sprinkle with Lee Kum Kee Coarse Ground Black Peppercorns and cook over low heat until fully cooked. Remove from the pan and place on the serving plate. | Drizzle the remaining sauce over the salmon fillets and serve immediately. | Tips: To prevent the salmon fillets from sticking to the pan, sprinkle a small amount of salt onto the heated pan before adding the salmon.

coveo:metadata.title: Mango Lassi
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Fresh mango 1 piece; Milk 1/2 cup; Plain yogurt 1/3 cup; Mango puree 1/3 cup; Ice cubes 6-8 pieces; Sugar 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Add mango, milk, yogurt, mango pulp, cane sugar (start with less add more if needed), and ice to a high-speed blender. Blend on high until smooth. | Taste and adjust: add more sugar for sweetness, more pulp for mango flavor, or a bit more yogurt for tang. | Serve right away and enjoy! Or refrigerate, covered, for up to 24 hours.

coveo:metadata.title: Egg Skin Dumplings (Dan Jiao)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Green onion 3 stalks; Garlic 1 clove; Ginger 1 tsp; Ground pork 1/2 lbs; Soy sauce 1 tbsp + 1 tsp; Sesame oil 1/2 tsp + 1 tsp; Rice wine vinegar 1/2 tsp + 1/2 tsp; Ground black pepper 1/2 tsp; Chili flakes 1/2 tsp; Tapioca starch (or potato starch) 1/2 tsp + 1/2 tsp; Eggs 4-5 pieces; Water 1 tbsp; Chicken bullion 1 tsp; Black sesame seeds 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a bowl, combine pork mince, spring onion, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, rice wine vinegar, black pepper, chili flakes, and starch. Mix with a fork or chopsticks until it becomes a paste, then set aside. | Whisk together eggs, water, and potato starch until smooth. | Heat a lightly oiled ladle over medium heat. Pour in a spoonful of the egg mixture and spread it into a circle. When the bottom is set and the top is still slightly wet, add a spoonful of pork filling to the center. Fold the egg over into a half-moon shape and gently press the edges with a spatula to seal. Set aside and repeat until all the mixture is used. | In a steamer carefully add the dumplings, cover, and simmer for 8 minutes. | Make the dipping sauce. In a pan, add water, chicken stock cube, soy sauce, sesame oil, and rice wine vinegar. Stir and bring to a simmer. | Plate the dumplings, spoon over the sauce, and top with spring onion, chili, and black sesame seeds. Enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese BBQ Cumin Lamb Skewers
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Red chili flakes 1 tbsp; Cumin 1 tbsp; Fennel 2 tsp; Coarse salt 1 tsp; Garlic powder 2 tsp; Boneless lamb shoulder 2 lbs; Canola oil 1 tbsp; Shaoxing wine 2 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Grind chili flakes, cumin, and fennel. Add garlic and salt, then briefly grind to mix. Set aside 1 tablespoon of the spice mix. | Cut boneless lamb shoulder into 1-inch pieces. | Toss lamb with the rest of the mix, oil, and Shaoxing wine. | Thread the lamb tightly onto skewers, leaving a 3-inch handle and the tip exposed. | Grill skewers over medium high heat for about 4–6 minutes per side, until cooked through. | Remove from grill and sprinkle the extra spice mix over the skewers, then serve! | Tip: Soak bamboo skewers in cold water for at least 2 hours or overnight.

coveo:metadata.title: Indomie Spring Rolls
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Indomie Mi Goreng BBQ Chicken Flavor 1 pack; Rice paper wrapper 1 pack; Cucumber strips (remove seeds) 1 piece; Cilantro 1 bunch; Turkey Spam 1 can; Sweet Chili Sauce for dipping
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Boil the Indomie noodles for 3 minutes, drain, and mix with seasoning packets. | Prepare the fillings. Wash and cut the cucumber. Rise the cilantro. Slice and pan-fry the Turkey Spam until golden. | Soften the rice paper. Fill a shallow dish with warm water. Dip a rice paper sheet for 5-10 seconds until just soft. (don't over-soak) Lay it flat on a clean surface. | Assemble the rolls. In the center of the rice paper, layer a piece of Turkey Spam, a few cucumber strips, cilantro, and Indomie noodles. Fold in the sides, then roll tightly from the bottom like a burrito. Serve with a side of sweet chili sauce.

coveo:metadata.title: Cambodian Black Pepper Beef
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Steak (cut into 1½ inch pieces) 1 lbs; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp; Red onion 1 piece; Tomato 1 piece; Lettuce 1 bunch; Steamed rice 1-2 cups; Oyster sauce 1 tbsp + 2 tbsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp + 1 tbsp; Dark soy sauce 2 tsp; Black pepper 1 tsp + 1 tsp; Cornstarch 1 tsp; Baking soda 1/2 tsp; Garlic 1-3 cloves; Sugar 1 tsp; Lime juice 1 tsp; Salt 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a medium bowl, combine 1 tablespoon oyster sauce, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 2 teaspoons sweet dark soy sauce, 1 teaspoon ground Kampot pepper, 1 teaspoon cornflour (cornstarch), ½ teaspoon baking soda, and 2 finely grated garlic cloves. Mix well until the cornflour and baking soda are fully dissolved and the marinade is smooth and evenly blended. | To make the stir-fry sauce, mix together 2 tablespoons oyster sauce, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, and 1 teaspoon sugar. Stir until the sugar is fully dissolved and the sauce is smooth and well combined. Set aside for later. | In a small bowl, combine 3 tablespoons lime juice, 1 teaspoon ground Kampot pepper, and 1 teaspoon sea salt. Stir well until the salt is mostly dissolved. Set aside until ready to serve. | Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large pan over high heat. Add beef in a single layer and sear for 30 seconds, then toss. Repeat 3 times until lightly charred. Add onions and toss briefly, then pour in the stir-fry sauce. Toss for 30 seconds (or longer for well-done). Turn off heat and let rest in the pan for 1 minute. | Arrange lettuce on a serving plate, top with tomato slices and beef. Serve with steamed rice and dipping sauce.

coveo:metadata.title: Indonesian Fried Rice (Nasi Goreng)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken breast 1/2 lbs; Vegetable oil 1 1/2 tbsp; Kecap manis (sweet soy sauce) 1 tbsp + 2 tbsp; Garlic 2-3 cloves; Red chili 1 piece; Onion 1 piece; Cooked white rice 3-4 cups; Shrimp paste 2 tsp; Eggs 4 pieces; Green onion 2 stalks; Tomato 1 piece; Cucumber 1 piece; Fried shallots for garnish; Lime wedge 1-2 pieces
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Heat oil in a large pan or wok over high heat. | Add the chili and garlic, stirring for about 10 seconds until fragrant, then add the onion and sauté for 1 minute. | Next add the chicken and cook until it starts to turn white. Stir in 1 tablespoon of kecap manis and continue cooking for another minute, or until the chicken is nearly cooked through and lightly caramelized. | Add the cooked white rice, 2 tablespoons of kecap manis, and shrimp paste (if using). Stir continuously for about 2 minutes, until the sauce thickens and the rice begins to caramelize—this step is essential for flavor! | Garnish with green onions, red chili, fried shallots, and fried eggs. Serve with lime wedges, tomato, and cucumber, then enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Taiwanese Popcorn Chicken
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Chicken thighs 1 lbs; Soy sauce 2 tbsp; Ginger (minced) 1 tbsp; Garlic (minced) 1 tbsp; White pepper powder 1/2 tsp + 1/2 tsp; Shaoxing wine 1/2 tbsp; Egg whites 1 egg; Sweet potato flour 2 cups; Five spice powder 1/2 tsp; Salt 1/2 tsp; Sugar 1/2 tsp; Basil 1 bunch; Vegetable oil 2 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut 1 lb of chicken thighs into uniform 2-inch pieces. | Marinate with soy sauce, grated ginger, grated garlic, white pepper, Shaoxing wine, and egg white. Mix well and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 24 hours. | In a small bowl, combine five spice powder, white pepper, salt, and sugar to make the seasoning; set aside. | Coat the marinated chicken evenly in sweet potato flour, ensuring no spots are left bare. | Fry at 350°F for 3–4 minutes, then add fresh basil and cook for an additional 30 seconds. | Sprinkle with the seasoning immediately after frying, and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Taiwanese Dan Bing (Egg Pancake)
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: All purpose flour 1/2 cup; Water 2/3 cup; Salt 1/4 tsp; Green onion 1 bunch; Egg 3-4 pieces; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: In a medium bowl mix together the flour, water, and salt until smooth with no lumps. The batter should be very thin consistency. Set the batter aside and let the mixture rest for 30 minutes. | Next, prepare the filling ingredients. Start by thinly slicing the green onions, then beat the eggs in a small mixing bowl, and set both aside. | Warm a large non-stick pan on medium heat and lightly brush with vegetable oil. | Pour 1/4 cup of the batter on to the pan and swirl around until the bottom on the pan is evenly coated. Cook for about 2 minutes then flip and cook the other side making sure that the pancake is lightly browned. Remove and set aside. | Then pour in the beaten egg and swirl the pan until there is an even layer of egg. While the egg is still wet generously sprinkle green onion and place the pancake on top. Make sure to press the pancake into the egg so that it sticks together and cook for another minute. | When the egg is fully cooked flip it and carefully roll the pancake so that the egg is on the inside. | Slice into bite sized pieces and serve with soy sauce!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Boiled Peanuts
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Raw peanuts (in shell) 1 1/2 lbs; Water 8 cups; Sea salt 1 tbsp; Sichuan peppercorns 1 tbsp; Cinnamon sticks 1-2 sticks; Bay leaves 3 leaves; Star anise 2-3 pods
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Soak the peanuts in water for about 30 minutes to loosen any dirt on the shells. Wash them thoroughly and drain. Repeat if needed until the water runs clear. | Using your thumb and index finger, gently press and crack open the pointier end of each peanut. This allows the brine to penetrate the shells more effectively. | Place the peanuts in a medium pot and add 8 cups of water, ensuring they are fully submerged. Stir in 1 tablespoon of sea salt (adjust to taste), 1 tablespoon Sichuan peppercorns, 1-2 cinnamon sticks, 3 bay leaves, and 2-3 star anise pods (use 2 for a milder flavor or 3 for stronger). | Bring the pot to a boil, then cover, reduce the heat, and simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour. This will yield slightly firm peanuts. For softer peanuts, extend the cooking time and taste periodically to reach your desired texture. | Turn off the heat and leave the pot covered so the peanuts continue to absorb the briny flavors. Allow them to cool to room temperature. For best results, let the peanuts soak overnight in the refrigerator and enjoy them cold.

coveo:metadata.title: Stir Fry Lotus Root
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Lotus Root 2 pieces; Black fungus (or wood ear mushrooms) 8-10 pieces; Red bell pepper 1 piece; Chicken stock 1/4 cup; Oyster sauce 2 tsp; Salt 1/2 tsp; Sugar 1/4 tsp; White pepper 1/4 tsp; Vegetable oil 2 tbsp; Ginger 1 piece; Garlic 3 cloves; Green onions 3 stalks; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Peel and trim 2 pieces of lotus root, then thinly slice them. Soak and prepare 8–10 pieces of black fungus (or wood ear mushrooms), and slice 1 red bell pepper into thin strips. Mince 3 cloves of garlic, slice 3 stalks of green onions, and thinly slice 1 piece of ginger. | Bring a large wok or pot of water to a boil. Add the lotus root, black fungus, and bell pepper, and blanch for 45 seconds. Drain thoroughly and set aside. | In a small bowl, combine 1/4 cup chicken stock, 2 tsp oyster sauce, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp sugar, and 1/4 tsp white pepper. Stir well and set aside. | Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in the wok over medium heat. Add the sliced ginger and cook for 30 seconds to 1 minute until fragrant. Add the minced garlic and green onions, and cook for another 20 seconds. | Add the blanched lotus root, black fungus, and bell pepper to the wok. Stir-fry for 1 minute, then pour 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine around the perimeter of the wok. | Mix the stir fry sauce well then add it to the wok. Stir-fry for another 20–30 seconds, until the sauce thickens and coats the vegetables evenly. | Transfer to a serving plate and enjoy while hot!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Chicken & Mushroom Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Dried shitake mushroom 1 1/2 cups; Water 8 cups; Dried goji berry 2 tbsp; Dried red Chinese dates 6 pieces; Whole chicken (cut into pieces) 3 lbs; Vegetable oil 1 tbsp; Ginger (sliced) 1 piece; Shaoxing wine 3 tbsp; Salt 2 tsp; Green onion 4 stalks
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Wash 1½ cups of dried shiitake mushrooms a couple of times and rinse thoroughly. Soak them in 8 cups of water in a soup pot for at least 3 hours or overnight. After soaking, cut the stems and return the mushrooms to the pot of soaking water. | Add 2 tablespoons of dried goji berries and 6 pieces of dried red Chinese dates to the soup pot (no need to soak these beforehand). Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then immediately lower the heat to a simmer. | While the soup is simmering, wash 1 whole chicken (about 3 lbs) and pat it dry with a paper towel. Trim and set aside the chicken breasts for another dish. Cut the remaining parts of the chicken into large pieces and set aside. | After the soup has simmered for about 30 minutes, heat a wok over medium-high heat until it just begins to smoke. Reduce the heat to medium, then add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and 1 piece of sliced ginger. Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant. Swirl the oil to coat the wok, then spread the chicken pieces in a single layer. Lightly brown them on all sides, stirring occasionally, until most pieces are cooked through. Turn off the heat. | Add the browned chicken to the soup pot. Deglaze the wok with ½ cup of water, then pour the liquid into the soup pot as well. Add 3 tablespoons of Shaoxing wine, cover, and simmer over medium heat for another 30 minutes. | Season the soup with 2 teaspoons of salt, adjusting to taste. Garnish with 4 stalks of chopped green onion right before serving.

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Pumpkin Rice Cake
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Pumpkin 2 cups; Sugar 3 tbsp; Glutinous rice flour 3 cups; Vegetable oil 2 tbsp; Sesame seeds 1 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Steam a small pumpkin, and scoop out the flesh from the skin into a large mixing bowl. | Next add in the glutinous rice flour and sugar to the bowl, then mix well with your hands until a stiff dough begins to form. | Take about two tablespoons of the dough and flatten it out into a small pancake shape. | In a large nonstick pan add vegetable oil and turn the burner to medium low heat. Then carefully add in the pumpkin rice cakes and cook each side until golden brown. (about 2 minutes per side) | After the rice cakes have turned golden brown remove from the pan and serve with sesame seeds for garnish. | Let cool before serving, and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chinese Sausage Stuffing
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: French bread 1 loaf; Chinese sausage 3-4 pieces; Butter 1 stick; Brown onion 1 piece; Shallot 3 pieces; Celery 4 stalks; Garlic 3 cloves; Parsley 3 tbsp; Thyme 1 tbsp; Salt 1 tsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 2 tsp; Chicken stock 2 cups; Egg 1 piece
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut the bread into cubes and toast in the oven at 350F for 25 minutes or until golden brown. | While the bread is in the oven prepare the other ingredients. Slice the Chinese sausage, onion, shallots, celery, garlic, parsely, and thyme. | Heat a large pan on medium and cook down the Chinese sausage until the oil renders out. Then remove the Chinese sausage from the pan. | Using the same pan melt the butter and sautee the vegetables until everything has softened. | Season the mixture with salt, soy sauce, and sesame oil. | Next, add in the parsely, thyme, and Chinese sausage. Stir the mixture well and add 1 cup of chicken stock. Let mixture simmer for 5 minutes so that some of the chicken stock reduces. | Place the toasted bread cubes into a large bowl and add the mixture. | In a separate bowl mix together chicken stock and an egg, then pour over the stuffing mixture. | Pour the stuffing mixture into a greased baking dish and bake at 350F for 1 hour. | Remove from the oven and garnish with more parsley and thyme before serving, and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Sichuan Hot Pot Soup Stock
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Sichuan peppercorns 1/3 cup + 1 tsp for garnish; Dried chili peppers 1/2 cup + 8-10 pieces for garnish; Cinnamon sticks 2 sticks; Bay leaves 4 leaves; Star anise 2 pods; Cumin seeds 1 tsp; Fennel seeds 1 tsp; Cloves 1/2 tsp; Neutral cooking oil 3 cups; Garlic 6 cloves; Ginger 1 tsp; Green onions 5 stalks; Doubanjiang (Spicy Fermented Bean Paste) 1/2 cup; Fermented black beans (can use paste or sauce) 3 tbsp; White sugar 1 tsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Chicken bouillon 3 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Blend the dried chili peppers, sichuan peppercorns, cumin seeds, cloves, fennel seeds, star anise, bay leaves, and cinnamon sticks until they’re small flakes (not a powder). | Heat oil over low heat until gently hot (a tiny piece of garlic should sizzle softly). Add garlic, ginger, and green onion. Cook for about 2 minutes until fragrant, not browned. | Stir in the doubanjiang and fermented black beans. Cook for 1 minute. | Add the blended spices. Cook for about 10 minutes, stirring often, until the oil turns a bright orange-red. | Add sugar, Shaoxing wine, and Chicken bouillon. Stir to dissolve. | Remove from heat and let the mixture cool for 10–20 minutes. | Pour into a heatproof container. Top with whole dried chilis and crushed peppercorns. Cover and freeze overnight. | Once frozen, cut into 4 pieces. Store each piece in a zip-top bag in the freezer for up to 6 months. One piece = one small hot pot (or one side of a divided pot). | **To use the hot pot stock, add it to 4-5 cups of water (or chicken broth). Heat and stir to dissolve the soup base, then you’re ready to cook when it's boiling!**

coveo:metadata.title: Strawberry Santa Tanghulu
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Strawberries 1 pack; Bananas 2 fruits; White sugar 2 cups; Water 1 cup; Black sesame seeds 1/4 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cut off about ⅓ of the strawberry—this pointed piece will be Santa's hat. | Layer the skewer with the strawberry base, a slice of banana, then top with the strawberry "hat." Add black sesame seeds for Santa's eyes. | In a small pot, combine sugar and water over medium heat, stirring until the sugar fully dissolves. Once dissolved, stop stirring and let it simmer for 10–15 minutes until the syrup turns golden brown. Watch closely so it doesn't burn. | To check if it's ready, dip a wooden chopstick into the syrup, then into ice water for 3 seconds. If it hardens and crunches when bitten, it's ready. | Turn off the heat. Dip the assembled fruit into the syrup, then immediately into ice water. Place on a plate and let it set—within 3 minutes, the sugar will harden and won't stick. | Once the sugar has hardened and cooled completely, it's ready to eat!

coveo:metadata.title: 5-Spice Peking Chicken
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Whole chicken 2-3 lbs; Five-spice powder 1/2 tsp + 1 tsp; Light soy sauce 1/4 cup; Dark soy sauce 2 tbs; White sugar 1 tbs; Ginger 6 slices; Honey 1 tbs
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Lightly dust the inside of the chicken with ½ tsp of five-spice powder. | Make the marinade by mixing together ¼ cup light soy sauce, 2 tablespoons dark soy sauce, 1 tablespoon sugar, 5-6 slices ginger, and 1 teaspoon five-spice powder. | In a large bowl or Ziploc bag, combine the marinade and coat the chicken. Marinate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight. | Remove the chicken from the fridge 30 minutes before cooking and stuff it with the ginger from the marinade. | Place the chicken on a tray with a rack (or crumpled foil balls if you don’t have a rack) for proper airflow. | Pour the leftover marinade into a small pot and simmer on low for 5–10 minutes. | Stir in 2 tbsp of maple syrup or honey to sweeten and thicken. Set aside for basting. | Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C). | Place the chicken in the oven. After 20 minutes, you’ll see oil drippings—use them to baste every 20 minutes for an hour. If no drippings, brush 1–2 tbsp of oil instead. | After an hour, switch to basting with the prepared sauce every 10–15 minutes until it’s used up (about 30 minutes). | Remove the chicken when it reaches 165°F (74°C), roughly 1.5 hours (depending on size). Let it rest 10–15 minutes before slicing. | Once cool, serve and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Jellyfish Salad
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Instant Jellyfish 1 packet; Salt 2 tsp; White sesame seeds 2 tbsp; Vegetable oil 3 tbsp; Garlic 2 cloves; Green onion 3 stalks; Red pepper flakes 1 tsp; Rice vinegar 3 tbsp; Soy sauce 1 tbsp; Sesame oil 2 tsp; Sugar 1 1/2 tsp; White pepper 1/2 tsp; Chinese celery 5-6 stalks
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Rinse the jellyfish under cold water. Toss it with 2 teaspoons of salt in a bowl, then transfer to a strainer set over the bowl. Let it drain for 10 minutes to remove excess water. | In a skillet, heat neutral oil and pour over the red pepper flakes, garlic, and scallion whites. Cook until fragrant and softened, about 1 minute. | In a bowl, mix the rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and white pepper until the sugar dissolves. Add the drained jellyfish and celery, then stir in the garlic oil until well combined. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. | Just before serving, toss the jellyfish with 1 tablespoon of the toasted sesame seeds. Top with scallion greens, celery leaves, and the remaining sesame seeds. Enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Gyudon with HonDashi
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Thinly sliced beef 1/2 lbs; Yellow onion (sliced) 1/2 cup; Green onions 3-4 stalks; Ginger (minced) 1 tsp; Shoyu 1 tbsp; Shaoxing wine 1 tbsp; Mirin 1 tbsp; Sugar 1/2 tsp; HonDashi 1 tsp; Water 1/2 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Prepare the vegetables. Thinly slice the onions, green onions, and ginger then set aside. | Heat a large pan over medium-high heat. Then add water, HonDashi powder, shaoxing wine, sugar, mirin, and soy sauce. Cover the lid and bring the sauce to boil. | Once the sauce is boiling, add the sliced onions, and ginger. | When the onion is tender, add in the thinly sliced beef and cook until no longer red. | Serve the meat and sauce over steamed rice. Top with extra green onion and enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Chicken Mei Fun
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Thin rice vermicelli noodles 8 oz; Vegetable oil 2 tbsp + 3 tbsp; Dark soy sauce 1/2 tsp; Chicken breast (strips) 1/2 lbs; Cornstarch 1 tsp; Water 1 tbsp + 2 tbsp; Oyster sauce 2 tsp; Shaoxing wine 2 tsp; White pepper 1/4 tsp + 1/4 tsp; Five spice powder 1/8 tsp; Ginger 3 slices; Shallots (sliced) 4 pieces; Carrot (shredded) 1 piece; Cabbage (shredded) 1 cup; Green onion 4 stalks; Light soy sauce 1 1/2 tbsp; Sesame oil 1/2 tsp; Salt 1/8 tsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Soak the dried rice noodles according to the package instructions until soft (they should no longer feel hard when you bite one). Drain well and shake off any excess water. | In a large bowl, toss the noodles with 1 teaspoon oil and ½ teaspoon dark soy sauce. Set aside. | In a medium bowl, combine the chicken, cornstarch, water, oyster sauce, Shaoxing wine, white pepper, five spice, and vegetable oil. Mix well and marinate for 20 minutes. | While the chicken marinates, prep the ginger, shallots, carrot, cabbage, and scallions. Have everything ready before cooking. | Heat a wok over high heat. Add 3 tablespoons oil, then add the ginger and chicken. Stir-fry until the chicken is just cooked through. Add the shallots and stir-fry for 10 seconds, then add the cabbage and carrots and cook for another 30 seconds. | Season with 1½ tablespoons light soy sauce, ½ teaspoon sesame oil, and ¼ teaspoon white pepper. Stir to combine. | Add the prepared noodles and scallions. Reduce heat to medium and stir-fry until everything is evenly mixed and heated through. Season with salt to taste then enjoy! | *** For firmer noodles, serve immediately. For softer noodles, add 2 tablespoons water, cover, and cook over low heat for 1 minute before serving.***

coveo:metadata.title: Red Bean Soup
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Dried red beans (Adzuki Beans) 2 1/2 cup; Dried lotus seeds 1/3 cup; Dried lily bulb 1/3 cup; Rock sugar 1/3 cup; Brown sugar 1 3/4 cup; Dried mandarin peel 2 peels; Salt 1/2 tsp; Water 6 cups
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Rinse the red beans under clean water, then drain. Rinse once more to remove any dust, then drain again. Cover the beans with fresh water and soak for at least 1 hour or overnight. Do not soak longer than 24 hours, or they may begin to sprout. Rinse the lotus seeds and dried lily bulbs. Cover them with water and soak for at least 1 hour as well. | Add 6 cups of water, 1/2 teaspoon salt, the soaked red beans, and 2 pieces of dried mandarin peel to a large pot. Bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce to low heat and simmer for 30 minutes. | Add the soaked lotus seeds and lily bulbs. Continue simmering on low heat for another hour. After about 1½ hours total, the beans should be soft and easy to crush between your fingers. For a softer texture, cook longer. For firmer beans, reduce the cooking time. | Add rock sugar and brown sugar. Stir until fully dissolved. Large pieces of rock sugar may take a little longer to melt. | To thicken the soup, either mash some of the beans directly in the pot or mix cornstarch and water in a 1:2 ratio until smooth, then slowly stir it into the soup. | If the soup becomes too thick, add a little water to adjust. Taste and add more sugar if needed. Serve warm, or refrigerate and enjoy cold later.

coveo:metadata.title: 8 Treasure Rice
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Glutinous rice 1 1/2 cups; Coconut oil 2 tbsp; Sugar 2 tbsp; Red bean paste 3-4 tbsp; Jujubes (red dates) 3 pieces; Golden raisins 1 tbsp; Pumpkin seeds 1 tbsp; Goji berries 1/2 tbsp; Walnuts 1-2 pieces; Dried chrysanthemum buds 1 tbsp; Cornstarch 1 tsp; Water 1 cup
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Cover the glutinous rice with about 2 inches of cold water. Soak for at least 3 hours, or preferably overnight. | Drain and rinse the soaked rice a few times. Mix in the coconut oil and sugar. Stir well until the sugar melts and the rice is evenly coated. | Arrange the toppings in the bottom of a wide heat-proof bowl after coating the bowl with more coconut oil. Begin with the walnuts in the middle, then jujubes, raisins, goji berries, and pumpkin seeds. | Spoon two-thirds of the rice into the bowl over the toppings. Gently press it down without disturbing the design. Create a small dent in the center and fill it with red bean paste, spreading it into a thin layer. | Add the remaining rice on top and smooth the surface. | Return the assembled bowl to the steamer. Steam for another 60 minutes. | While the rice is steaming, combine water, sugar, and chrysanthemum buds in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes. | Mix cornstarch with 1 teaspoon of water until fully dissolved. Stir the slurry into the saucepan. Continue cooking and stirring until the syrup slightly thickens. Strain into a bowl and discard the chrysanthemum buds. | When the rice is finished steaming, spoon a few tablespoons of syrup over the top. Let it rest for 10 minutes. | To serve, place a plate over the bowl and carefully flip it over in one quick motion. Remove the bowl and pour the remaining syrup on top. Serve warm.

coveo:metadata.title: Tofu Miso Soup with HonDashi
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Extra firm tofu 1 pack; Green onion 2-3 stalks; Hon Dashi 2 tsp; Water 3 cups; Miso paste 2 tbsp
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Press the tofu to remove the excess moisture. Then cut the tofu into small cubes and slice the green onions, then set aside. | Add the tofu and green onions to a medium sized pot, then cover with water. | Next, add in the HonDashi powder and bring everything to a low boil. | Reduce the heat to low and mix in the miso paste into the soup until it is well dissolved. | Serve while hot and garnish with extra green onions. Enjoy!

coveo:metadata.title: Salmon Ochazuke with HonDashi
coveo:metadata.poc_ingredients: Cooked salmon 1 fillet; Cooked short grain rice 1 cup; Crispy puffed rice (bubu arare) 1 tsp; Shredded nori 1 tsp; White sesame seeds 1/2 tsp; Wasabi 1/2 tsp; Water 1 cup; Mirin 1 tsp; Soy sauce 1 tsp; Salt 1/8 tsp; Hon Dashi 2 tsp; Green tea 1 bag; Green onion 2 stalks
coveo:metadata.poc_instructions: Prepare the salmon and rice. Remove the skin and bones from 1 precooked salmon fillet, then flake the salmon into bite-size pieces and set aside. Cook 1 cup of rice, following the instructions on the package. | Heat a saucepan on low. Combine water and Hon Dashi until everything dissolves, then steep 1 green tea bag for 2-3 minutes then remove. | Add 1 tsp mirin, 1 tsp soy sauce, and ⅛ tsp salt to the saucepan. Bring it to a boil and set aside. | For each serving, add 1 cup of cooked rice to a bowl. Top with flaked salmon. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of bubu arare (or crushed rice crackers), 1 teaspoon shredded nori, ¼ teaspoon toasted white sesame seeds over the top, and 1/2 tsp wasabi. | When you‘re ready to eat, pour the hot dashi green tea mixture over the salmon and rice until it's halfway submerged. Garnish with green onions and enjoy!
